title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: Follow workflow: https://lbm364dl.github.io/follow--workflow/ Follow tidyverse style guide: https://style.tidyverse.org/ Maximum line width 80 characters. code formatting, always run air format . committing. Install binary PATH. try format manually. Extract complex logic private/helper functions (prefixed .) early. helpers stateless receive necessary context via arguments. Functions short. Ideally 25 lines. Split large functions smaller ones meaningful names. Main exported functions come first file. Private helpers come end. Use snake_case column names tibbles. Names must readable self-explanatory — cryptic abbreviations like NEm, Bo, VS, GE. Prefer descriptive names like ne_maintenance, methane_potential, volatile_solids, gross_energy. Avoid carrying redundant name+code column pairs (e.g. area + area_code, item_prod + item_prod_code) intermediate computations. Integer codes sufficient join/group keys inside functions. Human-readable name columns added final output stage via dedicated lookup join. add name columns new functions lookup helpers exist. Always make sure rules document linters passed change code. Don’t use imported functions without namespace prefix (e.g. use dplyr::filter() instead just filter()). use @importFrom roxygen2 documentation. tidy data project. Exported functions must accept return tibble tidyverse. Private helpers (. prefix) may use data.table internally performance, must convert back tibble returning. Never use bare data.frame. argument validation use rlang functions (e.g. rlang::has_name() instead %% names()). error messages use cli::cli_abort() instead stop(). warnings (cli::cli_warn()) info. Escaped characters regex must double-escaped R strings (e.g. \\. \.). defining functions expect column names arguments, expect symbolic names (unquoted) use {{ }} (curly-curly) inside. must functions inside functions. functions top level; helpers private . functions. Always use native R pipes (|>). Make functions look like piped expressions. Avoid loops. Use vectorised operations, purrr, dplyr/tidyr. Exception: data.table private helpers may use loops iterating small fixed set (e.g. column names). Avoid function signatures > 5 arguments. Group related arguments named lists. Use tibble::tribble() small inline tibbles. Use stringr functions instead base R strings. Use .argument name grouping.

title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: Estimation functions admit one defensible method must expose method = (tier =) argument selecting among , like calculate_enteric_ch4(tier = ). default rigorous available method; simpler methods remain selectable user’s choice, sensitivity analysis, quantifying accuracy benefit sophisticated method. Methods alternatives, never silent fallbacks: chosen method recorded output column (e.g. method_<quantity>), coarser method used explicitly requested.

title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: Use roxygen2. document exported functions. Private functions (. prefix) can remain undocumented. Finish doc sentences full stop. First line = title (@title tag). Short, verb imperative form. @description, @param parameter, @return, @export, @examples. One space #'. Indent continuation lines two spaces. Variable function names must exceed 30 characters. Never use \dontrun{} \donttest{} examples. examples must run R CMD check. functions depend external/remote data take long run live, use example = FALSE parameter pattern: add example argument , TRUE, returns small hardcoded tibble::tribble() private helper R/toy_examples.R (run real function first, sample ~10 rows fixture). @examples block just calls my_function(example = TRUE). See build_primary_production() R/toy_examples.R reference. functions run fast small input, just provide self-contained inline example directly.

title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: functions tests testthat. One test file per R script: tests/testthat/test_scriptname.R. Access exported objects (functions datasets) via whep::name tests, bare names. dynamic access loops, use getExportedValue("whep", nm). Never use ::: getFromNamespace() exported objects. Use tibble::tribble(), pipes, dplyr::pull(), pointblank assertions. Take test_gapfilling.R example. Make helper fixtures reduce code repetition.

title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: NEVER guess hallucinate reference titles, authors, years, DOIs. Always verify actual source (web search, PDF, DOI lookup) writing bibliographic information. verify reference, say explicitly rather guessing.

title: CLAUDE.md — WHEP Package
text: PR must pass GitHub Actions checks: NSE variables must declared utils::globalVariables() R/utils.R. stats:: functions must use explicit prefix (e.g., stats::median()). Run locally: Rscript -e "rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck(build_args='---build-vignettes', args=c('---tests','--ignore-vignettes'), error_on='error')" Run locally: Rscript -e "lintr::lint_package(linters=lintr::linters_with_defaults(object_usage_linter=NULL, line_length_linter=NULL, indentation_linter=NULL, commas_linter=NULL))" mandatory, optional. attempt manually match air style – always run binary. air PATH, install : download https://github.com/posit-dev/air/releases/latest/download/air-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows) use posit-dev/setup-air (CI). run air format . repo root. running air, also run devtools::document() update man/ files. Air formats .R files: R/, tests/, data-raw/, etc. just files edited. Every documented topic (functions datasets roxygen docs) must appear _pkgdown.yml reference: section. includes .Rd files man/. adding new exported functions documented datasets, add appropriate section _pkgdown.yml (create new section). verify: compare ls man/*.Rd topics _pkgdown.yml contents. Every .Rd file (except whep-package.Rd) must matching entry. Run locally: Rscript -e "pkgdown::build_reference_index()" Tests: devtools::test() — 2 pre-existing failures test_commodity_balance_sheet.R expected (pin format, skip_on_ci).

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: footprint analysis answers question: “much environmental pressure (land, water, emissions…) caused country ultimately driven consumption country B?” Modern supply chains global. Spain might import soybeans Brazil, feed pigs, export ham France. footprint analysis traces flows backwards entire supply chain, environmental cost growing soybeans attributed French consumer ate ham — Brazilian farmer grew beans. done building Multi-Regional Input-Output (MRIO) model, mathematical framework captures inter-industry inter-country linkages one system equations.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: whep package implements three steps: build_io_model() — Assemble MRIO tables supply-use data, bilateral trade, commodity balance sheets. compute_leontief_inverse() — Compute Leontief inverse, captures direct indirect supply chain linkages. compute_footprint() — Multiply Leontief inverse environmental extension data trace pressures final consumption. methodology follows FABIO model (Bruckner et al., 2019), builds physical MRIO tables global food system.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: diving code, key concepts:

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: supply table records much product process produces. use table records much product process consumes input. Together describe technology economy. whep, process belongs one four process groups, together cover transformations food system: process uniquely identified (proc_group, proc_cbs_code) pair.

previous_headings: Concepts and terminology > Supply-use tables and process groups
what: Mixed units in the supply-use system
title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: flows measured tonnes, live animals measured heads (number animals). matches FABIO convention. Different rows Z matrix can different units row’s entries compared row’s total output X[]. technical coefficients Aij=Zij/XjA_{ij} = Z_{ij} / X_j dimensionless ratios, system remains mathematically consistent. example, slaughtering process cattle: Use: 1000 heads cattle (heads) Supply: 400 tonnes beef + 50 tonnes offals + 30 tonnes hides (tonnes) cattle heads beef tonnes live different rows Z, unit.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: bilateral trade matrix records, product, many tonnes (heads, live animals) flow exporting country importing country. connects domestic economies global system.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: commodity balance sheet accounting identity product country: Production+Imports=Exports+Food+Feed+uses+ΔStocks \text{Production} + \text{Imports} = \text{Exports}   + \text{Food} + \text{Feed} + \text{uses}   + \Delta\text{Stocks} right-hand categories (food, feed, uses) form final demand — end supply chain products consumed rather used intermediate inputs. live animals (FAO include CBS data), whep constructs synthetic CBS entries primary production data: Meat animals (cattle, pigs, poultry…): production goes processing (.e. enter slaughtering process). Draft animals (horses, camels, asses…): production goes other_uses.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Z core MRIO model. square matrix row column represents (country, product) pair. Entry ZijZ_{ij} gives physical flow sector ii sector jj. example, row 5 (Spain, wheat) column 12 (Spain, flour), Z5,12Z_{5,12} amount Spanish wheat used Spanish flour mills. model constructs Z using industry technology assumption: Z=UMR×T Z = U_{MR} \times T : UMRU_{MR} multi-regional use matrix. starts single-country use tables expands trade shares, input can originate country. example, Spain’s pig farming uses 1000 tonnes soybeans, 60% Spain’s soybean supply comes Brazil, 600 tonnes attributed Brazil 400 domestic production. TT row-normalized supply matrix (transformation matrix). row sums 1 encodes process distributes output across products. converts process-level flows product-level flows.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: product, trade shares tell us country gets supply . Spain consumes 1000 tonnes soybeans, 600 come domestic production 400 come Brazil, trade share Brazil Spain 0.4 domestic share 0.6. shares computed bilateral trade matrices combined domestic production data CBS. used expand single-country use tables multi-regional system input can originate country.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Y final demand matrix. Rows (country, product) pairs (Z), columns (country, demand category) pairs. Entry YijY_{ij} tells us much product ii goes final demand category jj. demand categories derived CBS columns present data, typically: food, other_uses, stock_addition. Like Z, final demand expanded trade shares , example, food consumption wheat France can partly originate French wheat partly imported wheat.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: X vector XiX_i total output sector ii. construction: X=Z⋅𝟏+Y⋅𝟏 X = Z \cdot \mathbf{1} + Y \cdot \mathbf{1} meaning total output equals intermediate deliveries plus final demand.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: initial construction Z Y, model applies several corrections following FABIO methodology: Leftover redistribution: CBS reports processing seed use supply-use table fully account , unmatched quantity redistributed food category Y. prevents demand vanishing. Loss endogenization (optional, via endogenize_losses = TRUE): CBS contains losses column, losses moved Y diagonal Z. treats losses self-use within sector (commodity “consumed” production process) rather final demand. Diagonal rebalancing: diag(Z)≥Xi\text{diag}(Z)_i \geq X_i (diagonal accounts total output, usually due reporting errors), 80% diagonal value moved final demand 20% kept diagonal, proportionally distributed across sector’s demand categories. Negative output fixing: sectors Xi<0X_i < 0 (can arise trade data inconsistencies) corrected adjusting negative entries Y.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Leontief inverse L=(−)−1L = (- )^{-1} key analytical tool. technical coefficients matrix AA defined Aij=Zij/XjA_{ij} = Z_{ij} / X_j, representing much input sector ii needed per unit output sector jj. inversion, two corrections applied: Negative entries AA set zero: arise data inconsistencies distort inverse. Column sums AA capped 1: ensures (−)(- ) invertible even reported inputs exceed reported output. Leontief inverse captures total requirements — direct indirect — needed deliver one unit final demand. Entry LijL_{ij} tells us: “deliver one extra unit product jj final consumers, much total output sector ii need produce (including intermediate steps)?” makes footprint tracing possible. consumer buying bread France triggers just French bakeries, also French mills, French wheat farmers, Brazilian soybean exporters (feed involved), . Leontief inverse encodes ripple effects single matrix.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: environmental extension pressure want trace supply chain. Common examples: Land use (hectares): many hectares cropland sector use? Water use (cubic meters): much blue water consumed? Greenhouse gas emissions (tonnes CO2-eq): much sector emit? Nitrogen surplus (tonnes N): much excess nitrogen enters environment? provide vector ee one value per (country, product) sector.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: footprint matrix computed : FP=f̂⋅L×Y FP = \hat{f} \cdot L \times Y f̂\hat{f} diagonal matrix extension intensities fi=ei/Xif_i = e_i / X_i (pressure per unit output). entry FPijFP_{ij} tells us: “much environmental pressure occurring sector ii driven final demand category jj?” fd_labels provided, footprint uses FABIO diagonal approach: final demand column jj, function computes MP⋅diag(yj)⋅GMP \cdot \text{diag}(y_j) \cdot G, GG groups sectors item. decomposes footprint target item consumed demand category (food, uses, etc.). resulting tidy tibble decomposes origin country/product target country/product, giving full bilateral footprint map.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: simplest call uses default arguments — function fetches input data internally: downloads supply-use tables, bilateral trade, CBS data, builds IO model available years. restrict specific years: want inspect pre-filter input data, can build separately pass : endogenize losses (move final demand diagonal Z): result tibble one row per year. row contains: Z: inter-industry flow matrix (sparse). Y: final demand matrix (sparse). X: total output vector. labels: tibble mapping row/column indices area_code item_cbs_code. fd_labels: tibble mapping Y column area_code (consuming country) fd_col (demand category). can inspect single year:
code: library(whep)  io <- build_io_model() io <- build_io_model(years = c(2010, 2015)) supply_use <- build_supply_use() bilateral_trade <- get_bilateral_trade() cbs <- get_wide_cbs()  io <- build_io_model(supply_use, bilateral_trade, cbs, years = 2010) io <- build_io_model(endogenize_losses = TRUE) # Extract matrices for one year z_mat <- io$Z[[1]] y_mat <- io$Y[[1]] x_vec <- io$X[[1]] labels <- io$labels[[1]] fd_labels <- io$fd_labels[[1]] selected_year <- io$year[[1]]  # Dimensions: (n_countries * n_products) x (n_countries * n_products) dim(z_mat)  # Label mapping labels  # Final demand column labels fd_labels

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: small systems (< 5000 sectors), can pre-compute L: inverts (−)(- ) matrix. Column sums AA internally capped 1 prevent singularity, negative entries zeroed.
code: l_inv <- compute_leontief_inverse(z_mat, x_vec) dim(l_inv)

previous_headings: Running the analysis > Step 2: Compute the Leontief inverse
title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: systems larger ~5000 sectors (e.g. 192 countries x 125+ products = 24 000 sectors), computing explicit Leontief inverse requires much memory (e.g. 4.8 GiB 25 000 sectors). cases, pass z_mat directly compute_footprint(), solves (−)x=Y(- )x = Y without ever materialising full L.

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Now provide environmental extension vector. must one value per sector, order labels. land use, build native land extension combining per-crop physical cropland (build_cropgrids_land_extension()) grassland (build_grassland_land_extension()):
code: crop_land <- build_cropgrids_land_extension(source = "cropgrids_fallow") grass_land <- build_grassland_land_extension() |>   dplyr::select(year, area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u)  land_use_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(crop_land, grass_land) |>   dplyr::filter(year == selected_year) |>   dplyr::select(area_code, item_cbs_code, hectares = impact_u)  # Match it to the IO model's label order extensions <- labels |>   dplyr::left_join(     land_use_data,     by = c("area_code", "item_cbs_code")   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     hectares = tidyr::replace_na(hectares, 0)   ) |>   dplyr::pull(hectares)

previous_headings: Running the analysis > Step 3: Compute the footprint
title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: pre-computed L:
code: footprint <- compute_footprint(   l_inv, x_vec, y_mat, extensions, labels,   fd_labels = fd_labels ) footprint

previous_headings: Running the analysis > Step 3: Compute the footprint
title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Alternatively, pass Z matrix compute_footprint() derive Leontief inverse :
code: footprint <- compute_footprint(   z_mat = z_mat,   x_vec = x_vec,   y_mat = y_mat,   extensions = extensions,   labels = labels,   fd_labels = fd_labels ) footprint

previous_headings: Running the analysis > Step 3: Compute the footprint
title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: fd_labels provided (recommended), result includes target_item (consumed product) target_fd (demand category like "food" "other_uses"). uses FABIO diagonal decomposition approach. fd_labels omitted, columns Y treated individual sectors. appropriate square Y matrices produce target_fd column. result tidy tibble columns:

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: output tidy tibble, can use standard dplyr operations answer questions: country’s consumption drives land use? much Spain’s land use driven foreign consumption? top bilateral flows? products drive land use food consumption?
code: footprint |>   dplyr::summarise(     total_land = sum(value), .by = target_area   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(total_land)) spain_code <- 203L  footprint |>   dplyr::filter(origin_area == spain_code) |>   dplyr::mutate(     destination = ifelse(       target_area == spain_code, "domestic", "exported"     )   ) |>   dplyr::summarise(     total_land = sum(value), .by = destination   ) footprint |>   dplyr::summarise(     total = sum(value),     .by = c(origin_area, target_area)   ) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(total)) |>   head(20) footprint |>   dplyr::filter(target_fd == "food") |>   dplyr::summarise(     total_land = sum(value), .by = target_item   ) |>   add_item_cbs_name(code_column = "target_item") |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(total_land)) |>   head(20)

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: useful sanity check: total footprint equal total extensions. pressure must attributed somewhere. Small differences can arise negative-output corrections column-sum capping technical coefficients matrix.
code: # These two numbers should be equal (up to floating-point tolerance) sum(footprint$value) sum(extensions)

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Since build_io_model() returns one row per year, can loop :
code: io <- build_io_model(years = 2010:2013)  land <- dplyr::bind_rows(   build_cropgrids_land_extension(source = "cropgrids_fallow"),   build_grassland_land_extension() |>     dplyr::select(year, area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u) )  all_footprints <- purrr::pmap_dfr(   list(     io$Z, io$X, io$Y, io$labels,     io$fd_labels, io$year   ),   function(z, x, y, lab, fdl, yr) {     ext <- lab |>       dplyr::left_join(         land |>           dplyr::filter(year == yr) |>           dplyr::select(area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u),         by = c("area_code", "item_cbs_code")       ) |>       dplyr::mutate(impact_u = tidyr::replace_na(impact_u, 0)) |>       dplyr::pull(impact_u)      compute_footprint(       z_mat = z, x_vec = x, y_mat = y,       extensions = ext, labels = lab,       fd_labels = fdl     ) |>       dplyr::mutate(year = yr)   } )

title: Environmental footprint analysis
text: Since year independent, can parallelise using furrr:
code: library(furrr) future::plan(multisession, workers = 4)  all_footprints <- furrr::future_pmap_dfr(   list(     io$Z, io$X, io$Y, io$labels,     io$fd_labels, io$year   ),   function(z, x, y, lab, fdl, yr) {     ext <- lab |>       dplyr::left_join(         land |>           dplyr::filter(year == yr) |>           dplyr::select(area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u),         by = c("area_code", "item_cbs_code")       ) |>       dplyr::mutate(impact_u = tidyr::replace_na(impact_u, 0)) |>       dplyr::pull(impact_u)      compute_footprint(       z_mat = z, x_vec = x, y_mat = y,       extensions = ext, labels = lab,       fd_labels = fdl     ) |>       dplyr::mutate(year = yr)   } )

title: Environmental footprint analysis

what: Who Has Eaten the Planet? The paths of food systems beyond the safe and just operating space (1850-2020)
title: Processing Agro-Environmental Data
text: Food production covers basic human need, simultaneously main driver anthropogenic environmental impacts. impacts resulted transgression, brief period since industrial revolution, planetary boundaries defining safe operating space humanity. rich research literature quantifies last 60 years’ fast, heterogeneous, often unfair development food supply related environmental impacts, depend agro-climatic factors, technology, trade flows, greatly changed different trajectories around world. However, developments lack integrated approach, poorly quantified 1961. WHEP bridge knowledge gaps, assessing “eaten planet” answering questions: environmental impacts food production since 1850? role trade food supply displacing responsibilities impacts? impacts related planetary boundaries, food supply inequality? highly ambitious goals addressed four objectives: Constructing consolidated global country-level annual database agricultural production management, using massive data collation combination modeling. Estimating environmental impacts: greenhouse gas emissions carbon, land, water, nitrogen, phosphorus spatially explicit, integrated, dynamic modeling. Calculating product footprints tracing along international trade chains. Analyzing observed trajectories safe just operating space, assessing drivers, impacts production consumption levels related fair healthy supply. ground-breaking research shed new light environmental history food, opening many new research frontiers, providing necessary information design fair sustainable policies. can also visit European project site.

title: Add WHEP polity codes to a table — add_polity_code
text: table data frame. code_column Name column containing numeric area codes. year_column Name column containing years. Set NULL force current/default mapping. polity_code_column Name output polity-code column. backcast_anchor First year reported (non-back-cast) FAOSTAT data, default 1961. Years matched polity active anchor year, WHEP's pre-anchor series back-cast onto anchor-year territory rather reported data-year borders. Set -Inf disable match strictly data year.

text: Distributes manure nitrogen, carbon volatile solids survive management (output apply_management_losses()) across cropland crops grassland, local agronomic cap explicit overflow path. collected/housed manure fills cropland crop's cap (weighted West-2014 receptivity default), surplus spills onto grassland grassland cap, residual beyond reachable sinks follows disposal_method. -situ grazing stream deposited grassland falls, uncapped. Carbon volatile solids ride along stream post-storage bundle ratio, mass conserved construction.

title: Allocate field-available manure to cropland and grassland by crop. — allocate_manure_to_land
code: allocate_manure_to_land(applied, gridded = list(), options = list())

title: Allocate field-available manure to cropland and grassland by crop. — allocate_manure_to_land
text: applied tibble apply_management_losses() least year, territory, sub_territory, stream, applied_n, applied_c applied_vs. gridded named list describing land surface polity: crops: tibble keyed year, territory, sub_territory, crop allocation weight (manure_n_receptivity "area_x_receptivity", crop_n_demand "crop_n_demand") cap basis (crop_n_cap, t N, "potential_uptake"/"realised_removal"; crop_area_ha "fixed_ceiling"). grass (optional): tibble keyed year, territory, sub_territory grass_n_cap (t N) grass_area_ha. grassland cap scaled f_n_tolerance footing uptake-based crop caps ("fixed_ceiling"). Absent grassland means grassland sink (cap zero). options named list method options: method ("area_x_receptivity" default, "crop_n_demand"), cap_method ("potential_uptake" default, "realised_removal", "fixed_ceiling"), f_n_tolerance (default 1.2, applied uptake-based caps), fixed_ceiling_kg_ha (default 170, EU Nitrates) disposal_method ("over_apply_local" default, "unmanaged_disposal", "retain_unallocated").

title: Allocate field-available manure to cropland and grassland by crop. — allocate_manure_to_land
text: tibble one row per allocation target: year, territory, sub_territory, land_use ("Cropland"/"Grassland"/"Disposal"/ "Unallocated"), crop, source_stream ("collected"/"grazing"), applied_n, applied_c, applied_vs, over_cap method_allocation, method_cap disposal_method provenance columns.

title: Allocate field-available manure to cropland and grassland by crop. — allocate_manure_to_land
code: applied <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~territory, ~sub_territory, ~stream,   ~applied_n, ~applied_c, ~applied_vs,   2020L, "ESP", NA, "collected", 80, 800, 40,   2020L, "ESP", NA, "grazing", 20, 380, 12 ) crops <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~territory, ~sub_territory, ~crop, ~manure_n_receptivity, ~crop_n_cap,   2020L, "ESP", NA, "barley", 6, 50,   2020L, "ESP", NA, "wheat", 4, 40 ) allocate_manure_to_land(applied, list(crops = crops)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 13 #>    year territory sub_territory land_use crop  source_stream applied_n applied_c #>   <int> <chr>     <lgl>         <chr>    <chr> <chr>             <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  2020 ESP       NA            Cropland barl… collected            48       480 #> 2  2020 ESP       NA            Cropland wheat collected            32       320 #> 3  2020 ESP       NA            Grassla… NA    grazing              20       380 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: applied_vs <dbl>, over_cap <lgl>, #> #   method_allocation <chr>, method_cap <chr>, disposal_method <chr>

title: Animal codes and classifications — animals_codes
text: Maps live animal CBS items livestock classifications, process codes, associated product items used livestock modeling.

title: Animal codes and classifications — animals_codes
code: animals_codes

title: Animal codes and classifications — animals_codes
text: tibble row corresponds one live animal CBS item. contains following columns: Item_Code: Numeric FAOSTAT item code live animal. item_cbs: Name CBS item (e.g., "Cattle", "Asses"). proc_code: Short process code used internally (e.g., "p092"). proc: Descriptive process name (e.g., "Asses"). item_cbs_code: Numeric CBS item code (often equal Item_Code). Farm_class: Broad farm classification grouping animal. One "Cattle", "Dairy_cows", "Monogastric", "Sheep_goats", "Bees", "Game". Item_product: Name primary product derived animal, applicable (e.g., milk dairy cows). Item_Code_product: Numeric FAOSTAT code associated product item. Liv_prod_cat: Livestock product category animal belongs . Graniv_grazers: Broad feeding behaviour classification. One "Grazers", "Granivores", "Bees", "Game". Livestock_name: Internal livestock identifier used across datasets (e.g., "Cattle", "Dairy_cows", "Asses"). Animal_class: Fine-grained animal class, including production type distinctions (e.g., "Broilers", "Hens", "Hogs", "Dairy_cows"). Item_FAOmanure: Name corresponding FAOSTAT manure management item. Item_Code_FAOmanure: Numeric code FAOSTAT manure management item. Cat_Labour: Labour category used labour-related analyses. One "Cattle", "Equines", "Dairy_cows", "Birds", "Small_ruminants", "Pigs", "Bees". Cat_FAO1: Top-level FAO category. Currently always "Animal". item_bouwman: Item name used Bouwman et al. livestock datasets.

title: Animal codes and classifications — animals_codes
text: Derived FAOSTAT data internal livestock classification work.

code: head(animals_codes) #> # A tibble: 6 × 17 #>   Item_Code item_cbs       proc_code proc  item_cbs_code Farm_class Item_product #>       <dbl> <chr>          <chr>     <chr>         <dbl> <chr>      <chr>        #> 1      1171 Animals live … p111      Othe…          1171 Cattle     NA           #> 2      1107 Asses          p092      Asse…          1107 Cattle     NA           #> 3       946 Buffalo        p086      Buff…           946 Cattle     NA           #> 4      1157 Camelids, oth… p095      Came…          1157 Cattle     NA           #> 5      1126 Camels         p094      Came…          1126 Cattle     NA           #> 6       866 Cattle, non-d… p085      Non-…           961 Cattle     Meat of cat… #> # ℹ 10 more variables: Item_Code_product <dbl>, Liv_prod_cat <chr>, #> #   Graniv_grazers <chr>, Livestock_name <chr>, Animal_class <chr>, #> #   Item_FAOmanure <chr>, Item_Code_FAOmanure <dbl>, Cat_Labour <chr>, #> #   Cat_FAO1 <chr>, item_bouwman <chr>

what: Apply IPCC manure-management losses to the collected manure streams. — apply_management_losses
title: Apply IPCC manure-management losses to the collected manure streams. — apply_management_losses
text: Nets nitrogen surviving manure management onto field, applying IPCC 2019 management-loss fractions collected/housed streams split_manure_management(): applied_n = n_stream * (1 - FracLossMS) FracLossMS = FracGasMS + FracLeachMS + EF3 + FracN2MS. grazing (pasture/range/paddock) stream deposited situ keeps full nitrogen (-situ soil losses belong soil stage). Indirect N2O reported labelled sub-flux already-removed volatilized leached nitrogen (N removed twice). Carbon applied field applied_n times post-storage manure C:N (solid/liquid/excreta value stream's management system), applied C:N reflects storage, fresh excreta; carbon volatile-solids storage losses follow .

title: Apply IPCC manure-management losses to the collected manure streams. — apply_management_losses
code: apply_management_losses(split, options = list())

title: Apply IPCC manure-management losses to the collected manure streams. — apply_management_losses
text: split tibble split_manure_management(). options named list. method selects loss method ("ipcc_2019_tier2").

title: Apply IPCC manure-management losses to the collected manure streams. — apply_management_losses

what: Assert that footprint conservation invariants hold. — assert_footprint_invariants
title: Assert that footprint conservation invariants hold. — assert_footprint_invariants
text: Build-time gate footprint result, intended use pipeline regression test. Aborts footprint -traces origin sector (embodied pressure exceeds source, never happen) global share untraced pressure exceeds max_rel_loss. regression silently loses inflates pressure fails loudly instead shipping.

title: Assert that footprint conservation invariants hold. — assert_footprint_invariants
text: footprint Footprint tibble compute_footprint(), origin_area, origin_item value columns. extensions Numeric vector environmental extensions per sector, passed compute_footprint(). labels Tibble area_code item_cbs_code mapping sector meaning, passed compute_footprint(). x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. max_rel_loss Maximum tolerated global relative -tracing (pressure never reaches final demand). engine's negative-zeroing column capping cause small amount, non-zero default.

title: Assert that footprint conservation invariants hold. — assert_footprint_invariants
text: Invisibly, one-row summary summarise_conservation(). Called side effect aborting violation.

title: Assert that footprint conservation invariants hold. — assert_footprint_invariants
code: z_mat <- matrix(c(0, 5, 10, 0), nrow = 2) x_vec <- c(100, 200) y_mat <- matrix(c(85, 195), ncol = 1) extensions <- c(50, 30) labels <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 1L),   item_cbs_code = c(1L, 2L) ) fp <- compute_footprint(   x_vec = x_vec, y_mat = y_mat, extensions = extensions,   labels = labels, z_mat = z_mat ) #> ℹ Computing footprint for 2 sectors. #>   2 sectors have non-zero extensions. #>   Final demand: 1 column. #> Sparse solve path (no dense Leontief inverse). #> Computing footprints... #> ✔ Footprint complete: 2 non-zero flows. assert_footprint_invariants(fp, extensions, labels, x_vec) #> ✔ Footprint invariants hold (1/2 sectors ok).

what: Balance input-output flows so the footprint conserves. — balance_io_flows
title: Balance input-output flows so the footprint conserves. — balance_io_flows
text: RAS-balance inter-industry flow matrix toward product-balance margin. row target intermediate use, \(u = X - \text{rowSums}(Y)\); column target keeps original column composition, rescaled total matches \(\sum u\). balanced system productive (spectral radius \(\) 1), \((- ) X = Y\) holds footprint conserves – case productive systems examples tests. Caveat physical agriculture: livestock feed conversion makes columns \(\) sum well 1 (many tonnes feed per tonne product), balanced system productive. Balancing faithful margins remove , traced footprint -traces rather conserving (real 2010 model, 15%). RAS therefore , , fix grassland -tracing; always verify check_footprint_conservation(). Pass result z_mat compute_footprint() large max_column_sum (e.g. 1e12) conserve_extensions = FALSE.

Works dense sparse (Matrix) inputs, staying input representation efficient small dense tables large sparse input-output systems. (core also balances bilateral trade matrices.) matrices negative entries signed GRAS variant needed implemented .

title: Biomass coefficients for crops and livestock products — biomass_coefs
text: tibble row corresponds one product item. contains 68 columns: Code: Item code (character), corresponding FAOSTAT production codes. Name_biomass: Item name used biomass accounting. Equiv: Reference equivalence item used coefficients borrowed another similar commodity (e.g., "Wheat" oats). Category: Broad commodity category (e.g., "Cereals, ", "Barley", "Vegetables"). BG_Biomass_kgDM_ha: -ground biomass kg dry matter per hectare. Root_Shoot_ratio: Ratio root aerial biomass (dimensionless). Product_kgDM_kgFM: Product dry-matter content kg DM per kg fresh matter. Residue_kgDM_kgFM: Residue dry-matter content kg DM per kg fresh matter product. Conventional_kgDM_ha: Conventional yield kg dry matter per hectare. Organic_kgDM_ha: Organic yield kg dry matter per hectare. GE_product_edible_portion_MJ_kgFM: Gross energy edible portion MJ per kg fresh matter. GE_product_residue_MJ_kgFM: Gross energy residue MJ per kg fresh matter (may character due source formatting). GE_product_MJ_kgFM: Gross energy whole product MJ per kg fresh matter. GE_residue_MJ_kg: Gross energy residue MJ per kg. kg_product_kg_aerial_biomass: Fraction aerial biomass product (harvest index, kg/kg). kg_residue_kg_aerial_biomass_FM: Fraction aerial biomass residue, fresh matter basis. kg_residue_kg_product_FM: Ratio residue product fresh matter basis. Carcass_to_LW: Carcass--live-weight ratio (livestock ; logical placeholder crop items). Edible_portion: Edible fraction product (kg edible / kg fresh matter). N_kgN_kgFM: Nitrogen content kg N per kg fresh matter. Lipids_g_kgFM: Lipid content g per kg fresh matter. Carbohydrates_g_kgFM: Carbohydrate content g per kg fresh matter. Calcium_mg_kgFM: Calcium content mg per kg fresh matter. VitaminA_microg_kgFM: Vitamin content micrograms per kg fresh matter. Edible_kgDM_kgFM: Edible dry matter kg per kg fresh matter. Edible_kgC_kgFM: Edible carbon kg C per kg fresh matter. Edible_N_kgFM: Edible nitrogen kg N per kg fresh matter. Edible_kgP_kgFM: Edible phosphorus kg P per kg fresh matter. Edible_K_kgFM: Edible potassium kg K per kg fresh matter. NonEdible_kgDM_kgFM: Non-edible dry matter kg per kg fresh matter. NonEdible_kgC_kgFM: Non-edible carbon kg C per kg fresh matter. NonEdible_kgN_kgFM: Non-edible nitrogen kg N per kg fresh matter. NonEdible_kgP_kgFM: Non-edible phosphorus kg P per kg fresh matter. NonEdible_kgK_kgFM: Non-edible potassium kg K per kg fresh matter. Product_kgN_kgDM: Nitrogen content product kg N per kg dry matter. Product_kgP_kgDM: Phosphorus content product kg P per kg dry matter. Product_kgK_kgDM: Potassium content product kg K per kg dry matter. Product_kgC_kgDM: Carbon content product kg C per kg dry matter. Residue_kgN_kgDM: Nitrogen content residue kg N per kg dry matter. Residue_kgP_kgDM: Phosphorus content residue kg P per kg dry matter. Residue_kgK_kgDM: Potassium content residue kg K per kg dry matter. Residue_kgC_kgDM: Carbon content residue kg C per kg dry matter. Residue_humified_kgC_kgC: Humification coefficient residue carbon (fraction residue C stabilised soil organic matter). MgDM_m3: Megagrams dry matter per cubic metre (bulk density proxy). Root_kgC_kgDM: Root plus rhizodeposit carbon kg C per kg root dry matter. Root_humified_kgC_kgC: Humification coefficient root carbon. Root_mass_kgC_kgDM: Root carbon mass kg C per kg crop dry matter. Rhizodeposits_mass_kgC_kgDM: Rhizodeposit carbon kg C per kg crop dry matter. Residue_C_N: Carbon--nitrogen ratio residue. Root_kgN_kgDM: Nitrogen content roots kg N per kg root dry matter. GE_Roots_MJ_kgDM: Gross energy roots MJ per kg dry matter. Rhizodeposits_N_kgN_kgRootN: Rhizodeposit nitrogen fraction root nitrogen. Fiber_g_kgFM: Dietary fibre content g per kg fresh matter. SFA_g_kgFM: Saturated fatty acid content g per kg fresh matter. MUFA_g_kgFM: Monounsaturated fatty acid content g per kg fresh matter. PUFA_g_kgFM: Polyunsaturated fatty acid content g per kg fresh matter. PUFA_n3_g_kgFM: Omega-3 PUFA content g per kg fresh matter. Iron_mg_kgFM: Iron content mg per kg fresh matter. Zinc_mg_kgFM: Zinc content mg per kg fresh matter. Magnesium_mg_kgFM: Magnesium content mg per kg fresh matter. Cadmium_microg_kgFM: Cadmium content micrograms per kg fresh matter. VitaminB12_microg_kgFM: Vitamin B12 content micrograms per kg fresh matter. VitaminD_microg_kgFM: Vitamin D content micrograms per kg fresh matter. Folate_microg_kgFM: Folate content micrograms per kg fresh matter. VitaminC_mg_kgFM: Vitamin C content mg per kg fresh matter. VitaminE_mg_kgFM: Vitamin E content mg per kg fresh matter. Flavonoids_mg_kgFM: Flavonoid content mg per kg fresh matter. Carotenoids_mg_kgFM: Carotenoid content mg per kg fresh matter.

title: Build commodity balance sheets — build_commodity_balances
text: Construct commodity balance sheets (CBS) raw FAOSTAT data. convenience wrapper chains three pipeline steps: .read_cbs() — read & reformat FAOSTAT CBS data. .fix_cbs() — processing calibration, trade imputation, destiny filling, final balancing. .qc_cbs() — flag data-quality anomalies.

title: Build commodity balance sheets — build_commodity_balances
code: build_commodity_balances(   primary_all,   start_year = 1850,   end_year = 2023,   smooth_carry_forward = FALSE,   example = FALSE,   historical_data = NULL,   .fixed_data = NULL )

title: Build commodity balance sheets — build_commodity_balances
text: primary_all tibble primary production, returned build_primary_production(). start_year Integer. First year include. Default 1850. end_year Integer. Last year include. Default 2023. smooth_carry_forward Logical. TRUE, carry-forward tails replaced linear trend. Default FALSE. example Logical. TRUE, return small hardcoded example tibble instead reading remote data. Default FALSE. historical_data Optional harmonized historical CBS production rows add CBS historical extension. May data frame path parquet/csv file. CBS-shaped rows provide year, value, one area_code polity_area_code, one item_cbs_code item_prod_code, preferably element. Production-shaped rows without element accepted production unit tonnes. Default NULL. .fixed_data Optional tibble structure output internal .read_cbs() |> .fix_cbs() steps. supplied, primary_all ignored pipeline skips directly .qc_cbs(). Default NULL.

what: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
title: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
text: Estimates time series quantity fixed set territorial boundaries — polities active ref_year — data reported changing historical boundaries data year. Country borders change time, raw constant-territory series: 1900 figure "Austria-Hungary" figure present-day Austria. function estimates one spatial reallocation (dasymetric areal interpolation): data year, value reported source polity spread polity's extent year across regular grid, weighted covariate density (e.g. gridded cropland population; uniform = plain areal weighting). grid re-aggregated ref_year target boundaries: target's estimate sum grid mass falling inside . Target territory covered source data year imputed — grid cells still carry covariate mass, filled donor intensity (value per unit covariate) rather left zero. fraction target's covariate mass imputed reported imputed_share, honest confidence signal. estimate good covariate: supply gridded surface used elsewhere WHEP spatialization (cropland crop output, population demographic series, livestock density animals). covariate = NULL method reduces area-weighted areal interpolation.

title: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
code: build_constant_territory_series(   data,   ref_year,   polities = NULL,   covariate = NULL,   resolution = 25000,   donor = c("regional", "none"),   crs_equal_area = 6933,   max_cells = 2e+06,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
text: data data frame reported values columns: year: integer data year. polity_code: source polity reported value (must active year carry polygon). value: numeric value (summed polity appears ). ref_year Integer. Target boundaries polities active year (start_year <= ref_year <= end_year). polities sf polity polygons polity_code, start_year, end_year geometry. Defaults get_polity_geometries(). covariate NULL (uniform density, .e. area weighting) function function(centroids_sf, year) -> numeric returning non-negative density per grid-cell centroid (centroids supplied crs_equal_area). resolution Grid cell size, metres crs_equal_area. Default 25000 (25 km). Smaller accurate slower. donor Gap-imputation rule: "regional" (default) fills uncovered target cells region-wide value-per-covariate intensity sources data year; "none" leaves zero (covered-). crs_equal_area EPSG code equal-area CRS used gridding areas. Default 6933 (NSIDC EASE-Grid 2.0 Global). max_cells Safety cap grid cells per year (default 2e6). Aborts source/target extent exceed (usually stray continent-scale target); restrict polities, coarsen resolution, raise . verbose Logical; emit progress/warnings.

title: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
text: tibble, one row per (ref_year-target, data year): target_polity_code, year value: constant-territory estimate (covered + imputed) covered: mass cells overlapping source data imputed: mass added uncovered cells imputed_share: covariate fraction imputed (0 = fully observed) n_sources: number source polities contributing year

title: Build a constant-territory time series for a reference year's boundaries — build_constant_territory_series
code: # Self-contained toy: two adjacent square polities. Only "P1" reports a # value in 1900, so when the series is rebuilt onto the boundaries active in # `ref_year` 2000 (both polities), "P2" is imputed from "P1"'s intensity. make_square <- function(xmin, ymin, side) {   sf::st_polygon(list(rbind(     c(xmin, ymin),     c(xmin + side, ymin),     c(xmin + side, ymin + side),     c(xmin, ymin + side),     c(xmin, ymin)   ))) } polities <- sf::st_sf(   polity_code = c("P1", "P2"),   start_year = c(1800L, 1800L),   end_year = c(2025L, 2025L),   geometry = sf::st_sfc(     make_square(0, 0, 2),     make_square(2, 0, 2),     crs = 4326   ) ) reported <- tibble::tibble(   year = 1900L,   polity_code = "P1",   value = 100 ) build_constant_territory_series(   reported,   ref_year = 2000,   polities = polities,   resolution = 50000,   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   target_polity_code  year value covered imputed imputed_share n_sources #>   <chr>              <int> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>         <dbl>     <int> #> 1 P1                  1900   100     100       0             0         1 #> 2 P2                  1900   100       0     100             1         1

what: Build per-crop physical cropland extension. — build_crop_land_extension
title: Build per-crop physical cropland extension. — build_crop_land_extension
text: Convert gridded crop harvested area per-crop physical land area aggregate commodity-balance items, producing land extension keyed (year, area_code, item_cbs_code) FABIO footprint model. gridded land-use pipeline (build_gridded_landuse()) distributes FAOSTAT harvested area across grid cells; per-crop totals therefore conserve harvested area, -counts multi-cropped land -counts fallow. function turns harvested area physical occupied land: "cropland_apportion" (default): within cell, cell's physical cropland (cropland_ha, LUH2) split across crops proportion share cell's harvested area. Per-crop physical area conserves physical cropland rather harvested area, capturing double-cropping (scaled ) fallow (resting land charged crops rotation supports) resolution grid. "intensity_divide": crop's harvested area divided cell multi-cropping intensity (mc_rainfed, mc_irrigated). Requires multicropping. Unlike single country-level cropping-intensity factor applied uniformly every crop, methods distribute physical cropland actual spatial pattern crop. Coverage note: "cropland_apportion" per-country crop total bounded LUH2 cropland layer, can -represent perennial plantation crops (e.g. oil palm, rubber) classified outside cropland; crops may receive less land harvested-area baseline implies.

code: build_crop_land_extension(   gridded_crops,   gridded_cropland,   items_prod_full = whep::items_prod_full,   method = c("cropland_apportion", "intensity_divide"),   multicropping = NULL )

title: Build per-crop physical cropland extension. — build_crop_land_extension
text: gridded_crops Tibble gridded crop harvested area, crop-level output build_gridded_landuse() (built without CFT aggregation). Must columns lon, lat, year, area_code, item_prod_code, rainfed_ha, irrigated_ha. gridded_cropland Tibble physical cropland per cell. Must columns lon, lat, year, cropland_ha. items_prod_full Crosswalk production items commodity-balance items. Defaults items_prod_full. Must columns item_prod_code item_cbs_code. method Physical-area conversion method. One "cropland_apportion" (default) "intensity_divide". multicropping Tibble per-cell multi-cropping intensity, required method = "intensity_divide". Must columns lon, lat, mc_rainfed, mc_irrigated (optionally year).

code: gridded_crops <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~year, ~area_code, ~item_prod_code, ~rainfed_ha, ~irrigated_ha,   0.25, 50.25, 2000L, 1L, 15L, 600, 0,   0.25, 50.25, 2000L, 1L, 27L, 200, 0,   0.75, 50.25, 2000L, 1L, 15L, 400, 0 ) gridded_cropland <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~year, ~cropland_ha,   0.25, 50.25, 2000L, 1000,   0.75, 50.25, 2000L, 500 ) items <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_prod_code, ~item_cbs_code,   15L, 2511L,   27L, 2805L ) build_crop_land_extension(gridded_crops, gridded_cropland, items_prod_full = items) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>    year area_code item_cbs_code impact_u method_land        #>   <int>     <int>         <int>    <dbl> <chr>              #> 1  2000         1          2511     1250 cropland_apportion #> 2  2000         1          2805      250 cropland_apportion

what: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
title: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
text: Convert FAOSTAT harvested area per-crop physical cropland using CROPGRIDS, return land extension keyed (year, area_code, item_cbs_code) FABIO footprint model. CROPGRIDS (Tang et al. 2024) reports, per crop country, harvested area crop (physical) area 2020. ratio genuinely per-crop multi-cropping correction — e.g. rice ~0.81 (heavily double-cropped), crops ~0.95-1.0 (single-cropped) — single country-level cropping-intensity factor, cell-level apportionment harvested share, reproduce. function applies per-(area, item) physical / harvested ratio WHEP harvested area year: \(physical = harvested \times (physical_{cg} / harvested_{cg})\). Note: CROPGRIDS physical area land crop actually grows; excludes fallow land (unlike fallow-inclusive cropland-apportionment), totals typically percent harvested area. Coverage: crops absent CROPGRIDS (notably FAOSTAT fodder items minor crops) physical/harvested ratio fall back ratio 1 (physical = harvested, multi-cropping correction). share harvested area hitting fallback reported via warning.

title: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
code: build_cropgrids_land_extension(   harvested = NULL,   cropgrids = NULL,   source = c("cropgrids", "cropgrids_fallow"),   max_ratio = 1.5,   min_cropgrids_ha = 100 )

title: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
text: harvested Tibble harvested area columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, harvested_ha. NULL, built get_primary_production() (unit == "ha"). cropgrids Tibble national crop areas columns area_code, item_cbs_code, physical_ha, harvested_ha. NULL, remote pin selected source read via whep_read_file(). source CROPGRIDS pin read cropgrids NULL: "cropgrids" (cropgrids-land: physical crop area, excludes fallow) "cropgrids_fallow" (cropgrids-fallow-land: physical area rotational fallow attributed crops attribute_fallow_to_crops()). Also recorded method_land. max_ratio Cap per-area physical/harvested ratio (default 1.5). CROPGRIDS occasionally pairs normal physical area near-zero harvested area minor/aggregate crops, yielding spurious ratio hundreds; physical area realistically exceed harvested fallow share, ratio clamped . min_cropgrids_ha Minimum CROPGRIDS harvested area (ha, default 100) per-area physical/harvested ratio trusted. CROPGRIDS leaves rounding stubs hectares marginal crop-country pairs whose ratio unreliable; floor crop falls global per-item ratio instead.

title: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
text: tibble columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u (physical land area hectares), method_land.

title: Build a per-crop physical land extension from CROPGRIDS. — build_cropgrids_land_extension
code: harvested <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~item_cbs_code, ~harvested_ha,   2000L, 33L, 2511L, 1000,   2000L, 33L, 2807L, 500 ) cropgrids <- tibble::tribble(   ~area_code, ~item_cbs_code, ~physical_ha, ~harvested_ha,   33L, 2511L, 990, 1000,   33L, 2807L, 400, 500 ) build_cropgrids_land_extension(harvested, cropgrids) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>    year area_code item_cbs_code impact_u method_land #>   <int>     <int>         <int>    <dbl> <chr>       #> 1  2000        33          2511      990 cropgrids   #> 2  2000        33          2807      400 cropgrids

title: Build livestock feed demand. — build_feed_demand

title: Build livestock feed demand. — build_feed_demand
text: demand_tier Demand-estimation tier. "ipcc" (default) uses IPCC Tier-2 energy model ruminant species, Bouwman feed-conversion ratios pigs poultry, Krausmann per-head intake draft species. "fcr" uses Bouwman / Krausmann magnitude every species. method actually used row recorded method_demand. Output grain. "category" (default) returns per-livestock category demand. "feed_type" splits across feed types returns feed_demand table redistribute_feed() consumes, two compose: build_feed_demand(= "feed_type") |> redistribute_feed(feed_avail). example TRUE, return small example output without downloading remote data. Default FALSE.

title: Build livestock feed demand. — build_feed_demand
text: = "category", tibble one row per (year, area_code, livestock_category): year: year demand. area_code: country code. code details see e.g. add_area_name(). livestock_category: feed-demand grouping livestock (e.g. Cattle_milk, Cattle_meat, Pigs, Poultry). demand_dm_t: Dry-matter feed demand tonnes. method_demand: demand method(s) used, e.g. ipcc_tier2_energy, bouwman_fcr krausmann_per_head (+-joined set mixed category whose animals used different methods). = "feed_type", demand split across feed types redistribute_feed() feed_demand contract: year, territory, sub_territory, livestock_category, item_cbs_code, feed_group, feed_quality, demand_dm_t, fixed_demand.

title: Build local (per-cell) feed intake, chunked by year. — build_feed_intake_local

NULL, bound result returned memory (practical years). demand_tier Demand-estimation tier, "ipcc" (default) "fcr". feed_mode Whether distribute surplus feed availability. "historical" (default) suppresses surplus-distribution pass: CBS feed element treated realised consumption, leftover availability dumped onto variable-demand livestock (inflate non-grass intake). "scenario" distributes surplus. overwrite Re-run years whose output file already exists. Default FALSE skips batch restartable. example TRUE, return small example output without sourcing remote gridded data. Default FALSE. run_dir Optional path finished local LPJmL output directory holding pft_npp.nc cftfrac.nc. NULL, pinned grass availability used unless grass_availability grass_availability_path supplied. input_dir Optional directory holding locally prepared spatialization inputs. NULL, pinned gridded livestock/spatial inputs used. grass_availability Optional already-derived grass availability tibble/data frame passed build_grass_availability_lpjml(). grass_availability_path Optional path already-derived grass availability artifact passed build_grass_availability_lpjml().

what: Build the native grassland land extension. — build_grassland_land_extension
title: Build the native grassland land extension. — build_grassland_land_extension
text: Produce grassland land extension keyed (year, area_code, item_cbs_code), replacing grassland rows used come external land_fp pin. Two area sources available, selected source: "luh2" (default): permanent temporary grassland area (item_cbs 3000 3002, LUH2 pasture rangeland) taken build_primary_production(). shares gridded LUH2 land-use basis used crop land extensions livestock spatialisation. Rotational fallow (item_cbs 3003) excluded cropgrids_fallow crop extension already attributes fallow crops, counting double count . "faostat_pasture": FAOSTAT "Permanent meadows pastures" area (Land Use item 6655), statistics-based basis comparable published footprint studies. Two metrics available, selected grassland_metric: "occupation" (default): full grassland area charged occupied land. "active_grazing": grassland capped area implied actual grazing intake ("grass" feed get_feed_intake()) divided usable grass yield, ungrazed marginal rangeland charged.

title: Build the native grassland land extension. — build_grassland_land_extension
code: build_grassland_land_extension(   source = c("luh2", "faostat_pasture"),   grassland_metric = c("occupation", "active_grazing"),   usable_grass_yield_dm_t_ha = 2.06,   data = list(),   example = FALSE )

title: Build the native grassland land extension. — build_grassland_land_extension
text: source Grassland area source, "luh2" (default) "faostat_pasture". grassland_metric Grassland land metric, "occupation" (default) "active_grazing". usable_grass_yield_dm_t_ha Usable grass yield dry-matter tonnes per hectare, used "active_grazing". Defaults 2.06. data Optional named list pre-loaded inputs avoid remote reads: primary_prod (source = "luh2"), landuse (faostat-landuse pin, source = "faostat_pasture") feed_intake (grassland_metric = "active_grazing"). falls back reader (get_primary_production(), whep_read_file(), get_feed_intake()) absent. example TRUE, return small fixture instead reading remote data. Defaults FALSE.

text: tibble columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u (grassland area hectares) method_grassland (chosen metric).

code: build_grassland_land_extension(example = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>    year area_code item_cbs_code impact_u method_grassland #>   <int>     <int>         <int>    <dbl> <chr>            #> 1  1986        10          3000 25000000 occupation       #> 2  1986       100          3000  8000000 occupation       #> 3  1987        10          3000 25000000 occupation       #> 4  1987       100          3000  8100000 occupation

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
text: Disaggregate country-level FAOSTAT crop harvested areas 0.5-degree grid. reproduces core spatialization workflow LandInG toolbox, adapted WHEP conventions tidy data structures. algorithm follows three main steps: crop's country total distributed grid cells proportionally spatial reference pattern (e.g. Monfreda) weighted gridded cropland extent (e.g. LUH2/HYDE). total allocated harvested area cell exceeds capacity (cropland times multi-cropping suitability), excess iteratively redistributed using logit-based transformation. Individual crops aggregated crop functional types (CFTs).

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
code: build_gridded_landuse(   country_areas,   crop_patterns,   gridded_cropland,   country_grid,   config = list() )

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
text: country_areas tibble country-level crop harvested areas. Expected columns: year: Integer year. area_code: Country code (numeric, matching WHEP polities). item_prod_code: FAOSTAT item code crop. harvested_area_ha: Total harvested area hectares. irrigated_area_ha: Irrigated harvested area hectares (optional, defaults 0). crop_patterns tibble per-cell spatial crop patterns. Expected columns: lon: Longitude cell centre. lat: Latitude cell centre. item_prod_code: FAOSTAT item code. harvest_fraction: Cropping intensity (Monfreda area divided reference cropland). gridded_cropland tibble per-cell cropland extent. Expected columns: lon: Longitude cell centre. lat: Latitude cell centre. year: Integer year. cropland_ha: Total cropland area hectares. irrigated_ha: Irrigated cropland hectares (optional, defaults 0). country_grid tibble mapping grid cells countries. Expected columns: lon: Longitude cell centre. lat: Latitude cell centre. area_code: Country code. Optional columns: cell_area_frac (area_frac): Fraction physical cell belonging polity compartment. Defaults 1. polycell_id, cell_id: Stable compartment/cell identifiers preserved outputs present. year validity intervals (valid_from/valid_to, start_year/end_year, from_year/to_year) historical, time-varying polity overlays. config Named list optional extras. Unknown keys raise error. Recognised keys: years: Integer vector years spatialize. NULL (default), years present country_areas processed. supplied, country_areas, gridded_cropland, type_cropland filtered set processing. cft_mapping: tibble mapping FAOSTAT items CFT names (item_prod_code, cft_name). NULL, CFT aggregation performed individual crop results returned. type_cropland: tibble per-cell, per-year, per-type cropland (lon, lat, year, luh2_type, type_ha, type_irrig_ha). provided alongside type_mapping, crop allocated cells containing LUH2 type. NULL, falls back total cropland. type_mapping: tibble (item_prod_code, luh2_type) maps crop LUH2 type. NULL, type-aware allocation disabled even type_cropland provided. multicropping: tibble per-cell multi-cropping suitability factors. Required columns: lon, lat, mc_rainfed, mc_irrigated. optional year column keys factors year (one row per cell per year); present, table filtered current year capacity constraint applied. absent, table treated static spatial layer applied every year. NULL (default), capacity constraint still runs mc_rainfed = mc_irrigated = 1 (harvested area capped physical cropland). max_iterations: Maximum iterations redistribution loop. Default: 1000L. expansion_threshold: Iteration number crops allowed expand cells without existing pattern. Default: 100L.

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
text: tibble gridded crop (CFT) harvested areas. Columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. year: Integer year. area_code: WHEP polity code cell compartment. polity_area_code, reporting_polity_code, reporting_polity_name, reporting_polity_has_geometry: Polity metadata area_code. polycell_id, cell_id: Preserved supplied country_grid. crop_name cft_name: Crop CFT identifier. rainfed_ha: Rainfed harvested area cell. irrigated_ha: Irrigated harvested area cell.

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
text: function reimplements spatial crop allocation LandInG toolbox (Ostberg et al. 2023, doi:10.5194/gmd-16-3375-2023) following extensions: LUH2 crop-functional-type constraints (type_cropland + type_mapping parameters) restrict crop cells containing LUH2 type (c3ann, c4ann, c3per, c3nfx). LandInG allocates total cropland without type constraints. MIRCA2000 crop-specific irrigated fractions (Portmann et al. 2010) irrigation distribution, falling back LUH2-proportional allocation.

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
text: Country areas: FAOSTAT QCL via build_primary_production Crop patterns: EarthStat / Monfreda et al. (2008) Gridded cropland: LUH2 v2h (Hurtt et al. 2020) Irrigation: MIRCA2000 (Portmann et al. 2010) + LUH2

title: Build gridded landuse dataset — build_gridded_landuse
code: # Minimal example with toy data country_areas <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~item_prod_code, ~harvested_area_ha,   2000L, 1L, 15L, 1000 ) crop_patterns <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~item_prod_code, ~harvest_fraction,   0.25, 50.25, 15L, 0.6,   0.75, 50.25, 15L, 0.4 ) gridded_cropland <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~year, ~cropland_ha,   0.25, 50.25, 2000L, 800,   0.75, 50.25, 2000L, 500 ) country_grid <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~area_code,   0.25, 50.25, 1L,   0.75, 50.25, 1L ) build_gridded_landuse(   country_areas, crop_patterns, gridded_cropland, country_grid,   config = list(years = 2000L) ) #> →   Year 2000: 2 rows (alloc 0.01s, cap 0.01s) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>    year area_code polity_area_code reporting_polity_code reporting_polity_name #>   <int>     <int>            <int> <chr>                 <chr>                 #> 1  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> 2  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> # ℹ 6 more variables: reporting_polity_has_geometry <lgl>, lon <dbl>, #> #   lat <dbl>, item_prod_code <int>, rainfed_ha <dbl>, irrigated_ha <dbl>

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
text: Disaggregate country-level FAOSTAT livestock stocks emissions 0.5-degree grid. species group uses tailored spatial proxy: Ruminants (cattle, buffalo, sheep/goats, equines): LUH2 managed pasture (pastr) plus rangeland (range), optionally weighted static manure-intensity reference (West et al. 2014). Confined animals (pigs, poultry): LUH2 aggregate cropland, reflecting intensive farming co-location crop production. Range specialists (camels): LUH2 rangeland . Mixed (animals): 50/50 blend pasture cropland. country, year, species group function distributes national total proportionally cell-level proxy weights: $$\text{cell} = \frac{w_i}{\sum_{j \\text{country}} w_j}       \times T_{\text{country}}$$ \(w_i\) proxy weight cell \(\) (land-use hectares times optional reference-pattern intensity) \(T\) country total (heads emissions).

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
text: Livestock spatialization covered LandInG (Ostberg et al. 2023), focuses crops . approach extends LandInG framework using LUH2-based spatial proxies (pasture, rangeland, cropland) livestock distribution. Country-level data comes build_primary_production() (stocks) faostat-emissions-livestock pin (CH4/N2O emissions), predecessor redistribution pre-1961 backfill already applied. Zenodo livestock density input (Heinke 2025, doi:10.5281/zenodo.14946695) provides alternative calibrated LSU/ha reference use glw_density parameter.

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
code: build_gridded_livestock(   livestock_data,   gridded_pasture,   gridded_cropland,   country_grid,   species_proxy = NULL,   manure_pattern = NULL,   glw_density = NULL,   grass_productivity = NULL,   years = NULL )

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
text: livestock_data tibble country-level livestock data. Required columns: year: Integer year. area_code: Country code (WHEP polities). species_group: Livestock functional-type name (e.g. "cattle", "pigs", "poultry"). heads: Live animal count (number head). additional numeric columns (e.g. enteric_ch4_kt, manure_ch4_kt, manure_n2o_kt, manure_n_mg) distributed grid using proportional weights heads. gridded_pasture tibble annual gridded pasture extent. Required columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates (0.5 degree). year: Integer year. pasture_ha: Managed pasture area hectares (LUH2 pastr). rangeland_ha: Rangeland area hectares (LUH2 range). gridded_cropland tibble annual gridded cropland extent. Required columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. year: Integer year. cropland_ha: Total cropland area hectares. country_grid tibble mapping grid cells countries. Required columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. area_code: Country code. Optional columns: cell_area_frac (area_frac): Fraction physical cell belonging polity compartment. Defaults 1. polycell_id, cell_id: Stable compartment/cell identifiers preserved outputs present. year validity intervals (valid_from/valid_to, start_year/end_year, from_year/to_year) historical, time-varying polity overlays. species_proxy tibble mapping species_group spatial proxy type: "pasture", "cropland", "rangeland", "mixed". Required columns: species_group: Group name (must match livestock_data). spatial_proxy: One "pasture", "cropland", "rangeland", "mixed". NULL, default mapping used (see Details). manure_pattern tibble static manure-intensity weights (e.g. West et al. 2014). Optional. Expected columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. manure_intensity: Relative intensity (kg N per ha similar). Values used multiplicatively land-use proxy. NULL, land-use weights used alone. glw_density tibble species-specific gridded livestock density GLW3 (Gilbert et al. 2018). Optional. Expected columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. species_group: Must match livestock_data. density: Heads per cell (reference year ~2010). provided, replaces LUH2-based proxy matching groups, still scaled LUH2 time trends. NULL, LUH2 proxies used groups. grass_productivity tibble grass productivity per cell (lon, lat, grass_npp) read_lpjml_grass_productivity(). Optional. provided, multiplies pasture/rangeland (grazer) proxy weights animals follow grass production rather area alone; cropland/mixed proxies unaffected. NULL, area proxies used alone. years Integer vector years spatialize. NULL (default), years present livestock_data processed. supplied, livestock_data, gridded_pasture, gridded_cropland filtered set processing.

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
text: tibble gridded livestock data. Columns: lon, lat: Cell centre coordinates. area_code: WHEP polity code cell compartment. polity_area_code, reporting_polity_code, reporting_polity_name, reporting_polity_has_geometry: Polity metadata area_code. polycell_id, cell_id: Preserved supplied country_grid. year: Integer year. species_group: Livestock functional type. heads: Allocated live animal count. additional numeric columns livestock_data (e.g. enteric_ch4_kt, manure_ch4_kt).

title: Build gridded livestock dataset — build_gridded_livestock
code: # Minimal example with toy data livestock_data <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~species_group, ~heads,   2000L,         1L,       "cattle",   5000 ) gridded_pasture <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat,  ~year, ~pasture_ha, ~rangeland_ha,    0.25, 50.25, 2000L,         600,           200,    0.75, 50.25, 2000L,         400,           100 ) gridded_cropland <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat,  ~year, ~cropland_ha,    0.25, 50.25, 2000L,          800,    0.75, 50.25, 2000L,          500 ) country_grid <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat, ~area_code,    0.25, 50.25,         1L,    0.75, 50.25,         1L ) build_gridded_livestock(   livestock_data, gridded_pasture, gridded_cropland, country_grid ) #> ℹ Spatializing 1 groups over 1 years #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>    year area_code polity_area_code reporting_polity_code reporting_polity_name #>   <int>     <int>            <int> <chr>                 <chr>                 #> 1  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> 2  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> # ℹ 5 more variables: reporting_polity_has_geometry <lgl>, species_group <chr>, #> #   lon <dbl>, lat <dbl>, heads <dbl>

what: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
title: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
text: Per-crop land occupation hectare-years (LCA m2*year convention): land-time crop's production ties , \(occupation_i = harvested_i \times L_i/12 + fallow_i\). first term active growing occupation — harvested area times mean cycle length \(L_i\) (months, MIRCA2000). uses harvested area, field double-cropped twice contributes cycles, long-cycle perennial contributes close full year. second term rotational fallow attributed crop, occupies land whole year rests. "active" occupation: land charged crop growing resting rotation, national total falls physical cropland area (also counts -season idle). distinct , complementary , physical-area extensions (build_cropgrids_land_extension()): measure field area crop holds; measures much land-time crop's activity occupies. Short single-cropped crops intensively double-cropped staples occupy less land-time per hectare long-cycle perennial crops.

title: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
code: build_hayr_land_extension(   harvested = NULL,   fallow = NULL,   season = NULL,   base = c("cropgrids_fallow", "cropgrids") )

title: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
text: harvested Tibble harvested area columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, harvested_ha. NULL, built get_primary_production() (reused build fallow term). fallow Tibble attributed rotational fallow columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, fallow_ha. NULL: base = "cropgrids_fallow" difference fallow-inclusive cropped CROPGRIDS physical extensions; base = "cropgrids" zero (growing occupation ). season Tibble mean crop cycle length columns item_cbs_code, season_months (strictly positive, unique keys). NULL, packaged MIRCA2000 season table used. Crops season given median cycle length. base "cropgrids_fallow" (default, include rotational fallow) "cropgrids" (growing occupation ). Recorded method_land <base>_hayr.

title: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
text: tibble columns year, area_code, item_cbs_code, impact_u (land occupation hectare-years), method_land.

title: Build a hectare-year (land-occupation) crop land extension. — build_hayr_land_extension
code: harvested <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~item_cbs_code, ~harvested_ha,   2000L, 1L, 2807L, 200, # rice, double-cropped (two harvests per field)   2000L, 1L, 2511L, 100 # wheat, single-cropped ) season <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_cbs_code, ~season_months,   2807L, 5,   2511L, 8 ) fallow <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~item_cbs_code, ~fallow_ha,   2000L, 1L, 2511L, 20 ) build_hayr_land_extension(harvested, fallow, season) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>    year area_code item_cbs_code impact_u method_land           #>   <int>     <int>         <int>    <dbl> <chr>                 #> 1  2000         1          2807     83.3 cropgrids_fallow_hayr #> 2  2000         1          2511     86.7 cropgrids_fallow_hayr

title: Build multi-regional input-output model. — build_io_model
text: Construct multi-regional input-output (MRIO) model supply-use tables, bilateral trade, commodity balance sheets. Uses industry technology assumption derive symmetric product--product tables. resulting matrices follow FABIO methodology (Bruckner et al., 2019). Rows columns Z represent (country, item) pairs. entry Z[,j] gives intermediate flow sector sector j.

title: Build multi-regional input-output model. — build_io_model
code: build_io_model(   supply_use = NULL,   bilateral_trade = NULL,   cbs = NULL,   years = NULL,   endogenize_losses = FALSE )

title: Build multi-regional input-output model. — build_io_model
text: supply_use Tibble build_supply_use(). default, function calls build_supply_use() internally. Must columns: year, area_code, proc_group, proc_cbs_code, item_cbs_code, type, value. bilateral_trade Tibble get_bilateral_trade(). default, function calls get_bilateral_trade() internally. Must columns: year, item_cbs_code, bilateral_trade (list-column matrices). cbs Tibble get_wide_cbs(). default, function calls get_wide_cbs() internally. Must columns: year, area_code, item_cbs_code, production, import, export, stock_withdrawal, stock_addition, plus final demand columns (food, other_uses). years Numeric vector years compute, NULL. NULL, computes years intersection available data across inputs. specified, must subset available years. endogenize_losses Logical. TRUE cbs contains losses column, losses moved final demand diagonal Z (self-use), following FABIO convention. losses column removed Y fd_labels. Defaults FALSE.

title: Build multi-regional input-output model. — build_io_model
text: tibble one row per year list-columns: Z: Inter-industry flow matrix (product--product). Y: Final demand matrix. X: Total output vector. labels: Tibble mapping row/column indices area_code, item_cbs_code, reporting polity metadata. fd_labels: Tibble mapping Y column area_code (consuming polity), fd_col (demand category, e.g. "food"), reporting polity metadata. Pass compute_footprint() fd_labels get target_fd column footprint output.

title: Build multi-regional input-output model. — build_io_model
code: su <- build_supply_use(example = TRUE) btd <- get_bilateral_trade(example = TRUE) cbs <- get_wide_cbs(example = TRUE) build_io_model(su, btd, cbs) #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf #> Warning: no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf #> ℹ Building IO model for 0 years. #>   Year range: Inf--Inf. #>   Final demand columns: food, other_uses, and stock_addition. #> ✔ IO model complete. #> # A tibble: 0 × 6 #> # ℹ 6 variables: year <dbl>, Z <list>, Y <list>, X <list>, labels <list>, #> #   fd_labels <list>

what: Build a consumption land footprint by physical trade balance. — build_land_balance_footprint
title: Build a consumption land footprint by physical trade balance. — build_land_balance_footprint
text: End--end land-balance footprint one year real WHEP data: assemble production (primary crop production plus grass dry-matter derived grassland area times yield), bilateral trade network, crop-plus-grassland direct-land extension, trace land consumers compute_footprint_balance(). independent, non-Leontief estimator stress-testing multi-regional input-output footprint via compare_footprint_methods(). Grass items (item_cbs_code 3000 3002) barely traded, land stays producing country: balance, unlike input-output model, route grass grass--livestock chain. production, trade extension supplied explicitly used -instead built, useful reusing cached inputs testing.

Default FALSE.

what: Build livestock nutrient flows from realised feed intake. — build_livestock_nutrient_flows
title: Build livestock nutrient flows from realised feed intake. — build_livestock_nutrient_flows
text: Top-level driver traces nitrogen, carbon volatile solids realised feed intake (redistribute_feed() result) livestock excretion, manure management application soil land use crop, plus management-loss side-streams. chains estimate_n_excretion(), split_manure_management(), apply_management_losses() allocate_manure_to_land(); "subnational" resolution additionally spills cell's un-placeable surplus neighbouring cells allocate_manure_transport() local disposal. Every method choice recorded method_* provenance column nitrogen balance (excreted = applied + management losses) conserved.

title: Build livestock nutrient flows from realised feed intake. — build_livestock_nutrient_flows
code: build_livestock_nutrient_flows(   intake,   resolution = "national",   methods = list(),   gridded = NULL )

title: Build livestock nutrient flows from realised feed intake. — build_livestock_nutrient_flows
text: intake tibble realised feed intake (redistribute_feed() result); see estimate_n_excretion() required columns. "subnational" resolution sub_territory "lon_lat" cell id. resolution One "global", "national" (default) "subnational". Transport cells runs "subnational". methods named list per-stage option lists, excretion, split, losses, allocation transport, forwarded matching pipeline function's options. gridded land-surface layer (crops optional grass tibbles) passed allocate_manure_to_land(); required default "potential_uptake" cap. NULL treated empty list.

title: Build livestock nutrient flows from realised feed intake. — build_livestock_nutrient_flows
text: named list applied (manure applied per land_use x crop (x cell) method_* provenance columns), losses (management-loss side-streams per polity) excretion (per-category excretion totals).

Must contain columns Item.Code (item_code_prod), Element, Unit, Year, Value, Area.Code (area_code). NULL, trade prices used. trade_prices tibble returned build_trade_prices(). NULL, computed internally. example Logical. TRUE, return small example tibble. Default FALSE.

title: Build primary item prices — build_primary_prices
