title: Build primary item prices — build_primary_prices
Build primary production dataset — build_primary_production
text: Construct full primary production dataset raw FAOSTAT inputs. convenience wrapper chains three pipeline steps: .read_production() — read & reformat FAOSTAT data. .fix_production() — apply Global-ported corrections. .qc_production() — flag data-quality anomalies.

title: Build primary production dataset — build_primary_production
code: build_primary_production(   start_year = 1850,   end_year = 2023,   smooth_carry_forward = FALSE,   example = FALSE,   show_duplicates = FALSE,   historical_data = NULL,   .raw_data = NULL )

title: Build primary production dataset — build_primary_production
text: start_year Integer. First year include. Default 1850. end_year Integer. Last year include. Default 2023. smooth_carry_forward Logical. TRUE, carry-forward tails replaced linear trend. Default FALSE. example Logical. TRUE, return small hardcoded example tibble instead reading remote data. Default FALSE. show_duplicates Logical. TRUE, return rows competing sources wide format (one column per source) diagnostic comparison. Default FALSE. historical_data Optional harmonized historical production rows add LUH2 historical extension. May data frame path parquet/csv file. Required semantic columns year, item_prod_code, unit, value, one area_code polity_area_code. Names item_prod_name, item_cbs_name, source used present; WHEP item area tables fill canonical names possible. Observed historical rows retained, LUH2 proxy filling can use anchors. Default NULL. .raw_data Optional tibble structure output internal .read_production() step. supplied, remote-data read skipped entirely pipeline starts .fix_production(). Columns required: year, area, area_code, item_prod, item_prod_code, item_cbs, item_cbs_code, live_anim, live_anim_code, unit, value, source. Default NULL.

title: Build primary production dataset — build_primary_production

Item names can recovered via add_item_prod_name() related helpers. show_duplicates = TRUE, returns wide tibble one column per source showing competing values.

title: Supply and use tables — build_supply_use
text: Create table processes, inputs (use) outputs (supply).

title: Supply and use tables — build_supply_use
code: build_supply_use(example = FALSE)

title: Supply and use tables — build_supply_use
text: example TRUE, return small example output without downloading remote data. Default FALSE.

title: Supply and use tables — build_supply_use
text: tibble supply use data processes. contains following columns: year: year recorded event occurred. area_code: code country data . code details see e.g. add_area_name(). proc_group: type process taking place. can one : crop_production: Production crops residues, e.g. rice production, coconut production, etc. husbandry: Animal husbandry, e.g. dairy cattle husbandry, non-dairy cattle husbandry, layers chickens farming, etc. animal_draught: Annual useful work energy generated draft animals. slaughtering: Slaughter live animals meat, edible offals, raw animal fats, hides/skins. processing: Derived subproducts obtained processing items. items used inputs non-zero processing use commodity balance sheet. See get_wide_cbs() details. process single input. processes like olive oil extraction soyabean oil extraction might make sense. Others like alcohol production need multiple inputs (e.g. multiple crops work), data process like alcohol production rather virtual process like 'Wheat products processing', giving possible outputs. constraint data obtained might improved future. See get_processing_coefs() details. proc_cbs_code: code main item process taking place. Together proc_group, two columns uniquely represent process. main item predictable depending value proc_group: crop_production: code item seed usage () reported commodity balance sheet, except field co-products process uses field aggregate item. example, rice production uses rice code, seed cotton production uses seed cotton code supplies cottonseed cotton lint. husbandry: code farmed animal, e.g. bees beekeeping, non-dairy cattle non-dairy cattle husbandry, etc. slaughtering: code live animal slaughtered, e.g. non-dairy cattle cattle slaughter. processing: code item used input, .e., one processed get derived products. uniquely defines process within group nature data used, can see get_processing_coefs(). code details see e.g. add_item_cbs_name(). item_cbs_code: code item produced used process. Note might value proc_cbs_code, e.g., rice production process row defining amount rice produced amount rice seed input, might also different value, e.g. row defining amount straw residue rice production. code details see e.g. add_item_cbs_name(). type: Can two values: use: given item input process. supply: given item output process. value: Quantity item's unit. items measured tonnes; live animals measured heads; animal draught measured annual useful work energy (TJ).

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: Performs LMDI (Log Mean Divisia Index) decomposition analysis flexible identity parsing, automatic factor detection, support multiple periods groupings. Supports sectoral decomposition using bracket notation summing grouping operations.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
code: calculate_lmdi(   data,   identity,   identity_labels = NULL,   time_var = year,   periods = NULL,   periods_2 = NULL,   .by = NULL,   rolling_mean = 1,   output_format = "clean",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: data data frame containing variables decomposition. Must include variables specified identity, time variable, grouping variables. identity Character. Decomposition identity format "target:factor1*factor2*...". target appears colon, factors , separated asterisks. Supports explicit ratios / structural decomposition []. identity_labels Character vector. Custom labels factors use output instead variable names. first element labels target, subsequent elements label factor order. Default: NULL uses variable names -. time_var Unquoted name time variable column data. Default: year. Must numeric coercible numeric. periods Numeric vector. Years defining analysis periods. consecutive pair defines one period. Default: NULL uses available years. periods_2 Numeric vector. Additional period specification complex multi-period analyses. Default: NULL. .Character vector. Grouping variables performing separate decompositions. Default: NULL (single decomposition data). rolling_mean Numeric. Window size rolling mean smoothing applied decomposition. Default: 1 (smoothing). output_format Character. Format output data frame. Options: "clean" (default) "total". verbose Logical. TRUE (default), prints progress messages decomposition.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: tibble LMDI decomposition results containing: Time variables grouping variables (specified). additive: Additive contributions (sum equals total change target). multiplicative: Multiplicative indices (product equals target ratio). multiplicative_log: Log multiplicative indices. Period identifiers metadata.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: LMDI method decomposes changes target variable contributions multiple factors using logarithmic mean weights. implementation supports: Flexible identity specification: Automatic factor detection identity string. Support ratio calculations (implicit division). Sectoral aggregation [] notation. Sectoral grouping {} notation. Period analysis: function can decompose changes single multiple periods. Periods defined consecutive pairs periods vector. Grouping capabilities: Use .perform separate decompositions different groups (e.g., countries, regions) maintaining consistent factor structure.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: identity parameter uses special syntax define decomposition: Basic format: "target:factor1*factor2*factor3" Simple decomposition (sectors): Basic: "emissions:gdp*(emissions/gdp)" Complete: "emissions:(emissions/gdp)*(gdp/population)*population" Understanding bracket notation: Square brackets [] specify variables sum across categories, enabling structural decomposition. bracket aggregates values calculating ratios. Single-level structural decomposition: "emissions:activity*(activity[sector]/activity)*(emissions[sector]/activity[sector])" Creates 3 factors: Activity level, Sectoral structure, Sectoral intensity. Multi-level structural decomposition: Two levels: "emissions:activity*(activity[sector]/activity)*(activity[sector+fuel]/activity[sector])*(emissions[sector+fuel]/activity[sector+fuel])" Creates 4 factors: Activity level, Sector structure, Fuel structure, Sectoral-fuel intensity.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
text: input data frame must contain: variables mentioned identity. time variable (default: "year"). Grouping variables using .. missing values key variables decomposition periods.

title: Calculate LMDI decomposition. — calculate_lmdi
code: # In these examples, 'activity' is a measure of scale # (e.g., GDP in million USD) and 'intensity' is the target # variable per unit activity (e.g., emissions per million USD). # The units are illustrative; adapt to your context. # --- Shared sample data --- data_simple <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~activity, ~intensity, ~emissions,   2010,  1000,      0.10,       100,   2011,  1100,      0.12,       132,   2012,  1200,      0.09,       108,   2013,  1300,      0.10,       130 )  # --- 1. Year-over-year decomposition (default) --- # Decompose annual emission changes into activity and intensity effects. # The additive column sums to the total change in emissions each period. calculate_lmdi(   data_simple,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   time_var = year,   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   period    component_type factor_label additive multiplicative #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 2010-2011 factor         activity        11.0           1.1   #> 2 2010-2011 factor         intensity       21.0           1.2   #> 3 2010-2011 target         emissions       32             1.32  #> 4 2011-2012 factor         activity        10.4           1.09  #> 5 2011-2012 factor         intensity      -34.4           0.75  #> 6 2011-2012 target         emissions      -24             0.818 #> 7 2012-2013 factor         activity         9.50          1.08  #> 8 2012-2013 factor         intensity       12.5           1.11  #> 9 2012-2013 target         emissions       22             1.20   # --- 2. Single baseline-to-end period --- # Pass a two-element periods vector to get a single cumulative period # instead of year-over-year results. calculate_lmdi(   data_simple,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   time_var = year,   periods = c(2010, 2013),   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   period    component_type factor_label additive multiplicative #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 2010-2013 factor         activity           30            1.3 #> 2 2010-2013 factor         intensity           0            1   #> 3 2010-2013 target         emissions          30            1.3  # --- 3. Year-over-year AND one cumulative summary period --- # Use periods_2 to append an extra comparison period alongside the # year-over-year results. calculate_lmdi(   data_simple,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   time_var = year,   periods = c(2010, 2011, 2012, 2013),   periods_2 = c(2010, 2013),   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 12 × 5 #>    period    component_type factor_label additive multiplicative #>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1 2010-2011 factor         activity        11.0           1.1   #>  2 2010-2011 factor         intensity       21.0           1.2   #>  3 2010-2011 target         emissions       32             1.32  #>  4 2011-2012 factor         activity        10.4           1.09  #>  5 2011-2012 factor         intensity      -34.4           0.75  #>  6 2011-2012 target         emissions      -24             0.818 #>  7 2012-2013 factor         activity         9.50          1.08  #>  8 2012-2013 factor         intensity       12.5           1.11  #>  9 2012-2013 target         emissions       22             1.20  #> 10 2010-2013 factor         activity        30             1.3   #> 11 2010-2013 factor         intensity        0             1     #> 12 2010-2013 target         emissions       30             1.3    # --- 4. Per-country decomposition with .by --- # Separate LMDI runs per country; results are stacked with a country column. data_countries <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~country, ~activity, ~intensity, ~emissions,   2010, "ESP", 1000, 0.10, 100,   2011, "ESP", 1100, 0.11, 121,   2012, "ESP", 1200, 0.10, 120,   2010, "FRA", 2000, 0.05, 100,   2011, "FRA", 2200, 0.05, 110,   2012, "FRA", 2400, 0.05, 120 )  calculate_lmdi(   data_countries,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   time_var = year,   .by = "country",   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     country,     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 12 × 6 #>    country period    component_type factor_label additive multiplicative #>    <chr>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1 ESP     2010-2011 factor         activity         10.5          1.1   #>  2 ESP     2010-2011 factor         intensity        10.5          1.1   #>  3 ESP     2010-2011 target         emissions        21            1.21  #>  4 ESP     2011-2012 factor         activity         10.5          1.09  #>  5 ESP     2011-2012 factor         intensity       -11.5          0.909 #>  6 ESP     2011-2012 target         emissions        -1            0.992 #>  7 FRA     2010-2011 factor         activity         10            1.1   #>  8 FRA     2010-2011 factor         intensity         0            1     #>  9 FRA     2010-2011 target         emissions        10            1.1   #> 10 FRA     2011-2012 factor         activity         10            1.09  #> 11 FRA     2011-2012 factor         intensity         0            1     #> 12 FRA     2011-2012 target         emissions        10            1.09   # --- 5. Ratio notation --- # Express factors as explicit ratios (e.g. intensity = emissions/activity). # Factor labels in the output preserve the ratio form for clarity. calculate_lmdi(   data_simple,   identity = "emissions:(emissions/activity)*activity",   time_var = year,   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   period    component_type factor_label       additive multiplicative #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>                 <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 2010-2011 factor         emissions/activity    21.0           1.2   #> 2 2010-2011 factor         activity              11.0           1.1   #> 3 2010-2011 target         emissions             32             1.32  #> 4 2011-2012 factor         emissions/activity   -34.4           0.75  #> 5 2011-2012 factor         activity              10.4           1.09  #> 6 2011-2012 target         emissions            -24             0.818 #> 7 2012-2013 factor         emissions/activity    12.5           1.11  #> 8 2012-2013 factor         activity               9.50          1.08  #> 9 2012-2013 target         emissions             22             1.20   # --- 6. Structural (sectoral) decomposition with [] notation --- # Decomposes emissions into: #   total_activity * sector_structure * sector_intensity # [] sums the bracketed variable across sector before forming ratios, # enabling proper structural decomposition. data_sectors <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~sector, ~activity, ~emissions,   2010, "industry", 600, 60,   2010, "transport", 400, 40,   2011, "industry", 700, 63,   2011, "transport", 500, 55 ) |>   dplyr::group_by(year) |>   dplyr::mutate(total_activity = sum(activity)) |>   dplyr::ungroup()  calculate_lmdi(   data_sectors,   identity = paste0(     "emissions:",     "total_activity*",     "(activity[sector]/total_activity)*",     "(emissions[sector]/activity[sector])"   ),   time_var = year,   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   period    component_type factor_label                  additive multiplicative #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>                            <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 2010-2011 factor         total_activity                  19.8            1.20  #> 2 2010-2011 factor         activity[sector]/total_activ…    0.191          1.00  #> 3 2010-2011 factor         emissions[sector]/activity[s…   -1.99           0.982 #> 4 2010-2011 target         emissions                       18              1.18   # --- 7. Custom factor labels --- # Replace raw variable names with readable labels for reporting. # Supply one label per term (target first, then each factor in order). calculate_lmdi(   data_simple,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   identity_labels = c(     "Total Emissions",     "Activity Effect",     "Intensity Effect"   ),   time_var = year,   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   period    component_type factor_label     additive multiplicative #>   <chr>     <chr>          <chr>               <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 2010-2011 factor         Activity Effect     11.0           1.1   #> 2 2010-2011 factor         Intensity Effect    21.0           1.2   #> 3 2010-2011 target         Total Emissions     32             1.32  #> 4 2011-2012 factor         Activity Effect     10.4           1.09  #> 5 2011-2012 factor         Intensity Effect   -34.4           0.75  #> 6 2011-2012 target         Total Emissions    -24             0.818 #> 7 2012-2013 factor         Activity Effect      9.50          1.08  #> 8 2012-2013 factor         Intensity Effect    12.5           1.11  #> 9 2012-2013 target         Total Emissions     22             1.20   # --- 8. Rolling mean smoothing before decomposition --- # A 3-year rolling mean reduces noise in volatile series before # computing LMDI weights. Edge years use partial windows (fewer # than k observations) so no periods are lost. data_smooth <- tibble::tibble(   year      = 2010:2020,   activity  = seq(1000, 2000, length.out = 11),   intensity = rep(0.1, 11),   emissions = seq(1000, 2000, length.out = 11) * 0.1 )  calculate_lmdi(   data_smooth,   identity = "emissions:activity*intensity",   time_var = year,   rolling_mean = 3,   verbose = FALSE ) |>   dplyr::select(     period,     component_type,     factor_label,     additive,     multiplicative   ) #> # A tibble: 30 × 5 #>    period    component_type factor_label additive multiplicative #>    <chr>     <chr>          <chr>           <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1 2010-2011 factor         activity            5           1.05 #>  2 2010-2011 factor         intensity           0           1    #>  3 2010-2011 target         emissions           5           1.05 #>  4 2011-2012 factor         activity           10           1.09 #>  5 2011-2012 factor         intensity           0           1    #>  6 2011-2012 target         emissions          10           1.09 #>  7 2012-2013 factor         activity           10           1.08 #>  8 2012-2013 factor         intensity           0           1    #>  9 2012-2013 target         emissions          10           1.08 #> 10 2013-2014 factor         activity           10           1.08 #> # ℹ 20 more rows

title: Calculate manure emissions (CH4 + N2O). — calculate_manure_emissions
text: Wrapper selects Tier 1 2 manure CH4 computes N2O (Tier 2 ; skipped Tier 1).

title: Calculate manure emissions (CH4 + N2O). — calculate_manure_emissions
code: calculate_manure_emissions(data, tier = NULL)

title: Calculate manure emissions (CH4 + N2O). — calculate_manure_emissions
text: data Dataframe species, heads. Tier 2, also needs cohort, weight, diet_quality. Tier 1, iso3 used select regional emission factors. tier Integer 1 2. NULL (default), auto-selects based data completeness.

title: Calculate manure emissions (CH4 + N2O). — calculate_manure_emissions
text: Dataframe input columns preserved, plus: method_manure_ch4: tracking label. Tier 1: manure_ef_kgch4, manure_ch4_tier1. Tier 2: volatile_solids, methane_potential, weighted_mcf, manure_ch4_per_head, manure_ch4_tier2. N2O (Tier 2 ): method_manure_n2o, n_excretion, manure_n2o_direct, manure_n2o_indirect, manure_n2o_total.

title: Calculate manure emissions (CH4 + N2O). — calculate_manure_emissions
code: tibble::tibble(   species = "Cattle", heads = 1000, iso3 = "DEU" ) |>   calculate_manure_emissions(tier = 1) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   species heads iso3  species_gen method_manure_ch4 manure_ef_kgch4 #>   <chr>   <dbl> <chr> <chr>       <chr>                       <dbl> #> 1 Cattle   1000 DEU   Cattle      IPCC_2019_Tier1                 1 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: manure_ch4_tier1 <dbl>

what: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
title: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
text: Estimates free-living associative BNF agricultural soils base rate (crop-specific bnf, default) scaled environmental modifiers nitrogen, temperature, water, soil organic matter, pH clay. modifier activates driver column present.

title: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
code: calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf(   x,   nonsymbiotic_params = list(),   soil_params = list() )

title: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
text: x tibble area_ha. Optional nonsymbiotic_base_kg_ha (item_prod_code join crop-specific base rate), plus nitrogen, temperature, water soil (som_pct, soil_ph, clay_pct) drivers. nonsymbiotic_params Named list overriding nsbnf_default_kg_ha, k_n_synth, k_n_org, t_opt, t_sigma, ai_threshold. soil_params Named list overriding k_som, som_ref, ph_opt, ph_sigma, k_clay, clay_ref.

title: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
text: input tibble nonsymbiotic_base_kg_ha, six f_*_nonsymbiotic modifiers, f_env_nonsymbiotic nonsymbiotic_bnf_t.

title: Estimate non-symbiotic biological nitrogen fixation. — calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf
code: calculate_nonsymbiotic_bnf(tibble::tibble(area_ha = 40)) #> # A tibble: 1 × 18 #>   area_ha n_synth_kg_ha n_org_kg_ha temp_c pet_mm som_pct soil_ph clay_pct #>     <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1      40             0           0     NA     NA      NA      NA       NA #> # ℹ 10 more variables: water_input_mm <dbl>, nonsymbiotic_base_kg_ha <dbl>, #> #   f_nitrogen_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, f_temperature_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, #> #   f_water_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, f_som_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, #> #   f_ph_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, f_clay_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, #> #   f_env_nonsymbiotic <dbl>, nonsymbiotic_bnf_t <dbl>

what: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
title: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
text: Converts crop NPP components (product, residue, root) weed biomass nitrogen carbon using bio_coefs per-component coefficients weed_coefs scalars. Root weed -ground nitrogen include rhizodeposits. Root carbon uses root_c_kgdm, includes root biomass carbon plus rhizodeposit carbon per tonne root dry matter; use root_mass_c_kgdm bio_coefs root tissue carbon alone. residue--soil split computed residue_soil_dm_t column (calculate_residue_destinies()) present.

title: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
code: calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen(x)

title: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
text: x tibble item_prod_code, product_dm_t, residue_dm_t root_dm_t. Optional weed_ag_dm_t (-ground weed dry matter; treated 0 absent), crop_npp_dm_t (kept present) residue_soil_dm_t (enables soil-residue nitrogen carbon split).

title: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
text: input tibble weed dry matter, nitrogen (*_n_t) carbon (*_c_t) product, residue, root, weeds, crop NPP total NPP.

title: Partition crop and weed NPP into dry matter, carbon and nitrogen. — calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen
code: tibble::tibble(item_prod_code = "15", production_t = 100, area_ha = 40) |>   calculate_crop_npp() |>   calculate_npp_carbon_nitrogen() #> # A tibble: 1 × 31 #>   item_prod_code production_t area_ha product_dm_t yield_dm_t_ha residue_dm_t #>   <chr>                 <dbl>   <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 15                      100      40         87.9          2.20         136. #> # ℹ 25 more variables: method_residue <chr>, root_dm_t <dbl>, #> #   method_root <chr>, crop_npp_dm_t <dbl>, weed_ag_dm_t <dbl>, #> #   weed_bg_dm_t <dbl>, weed_npp_dm_t <dbl>, weed_ag_n_t <dbl>, #> #   weed_bg_n_t <dbl>, weed_npp_n_t <dbl>, weed_ag_c_t <dbl>, #> #   weed_bg_c_t <dbl>, weed_npp_c_t <dbl>, total_npp_dm_t <dbl>, #> #   product_n_t <dbl>, residue_n_t <dbl>, root_n_t <dbl>, crop_npp_n_t <dbl>, #> #   total_npp_n_t <dbl>, product_c_t <dbl>, residue_c_t <dbl>, …

what: N soil inputs and Nitrogen Use Efficiency (NUE) for crop — calculate_nue_crops

text: N inputs (deposition, fixation, synthetic fertilizers, urban sources, manure) N production Spain 1860 present GRAFS model provincial level. crop NUE defined percentage produced nitrogen relative total nitrogen inputs soil. title: NUE for Livestock — calculate_nue_livestock
text: Calculates Nitrogen Use Efficiency (NUE) livestock categories (excluding pets). livestock NUE defined percentage nitrogen livestock products relative nitrogen feed intake: nue = prod_n / feed_n * 100 Additionally, mass balance calculated check recovery N products excretion relative feed intake: mass_balance = (prod_n + excretion_n) / feed_n

title: NUE for Livestock — calculate_nue_livestock
code: calculate_nue_livestock(example = FALSE)

text: example TRUE, return small example output without downloading remote data. Default FALSE.

what: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
title: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
text: Estimates potential net primary production per unit area, used downstream scale weed biomass cropland. Several methods available; default lpjml reads gross managed-grassland NPP finished LPJmL run, climate methods compute potential NPP temperature, water input actual evapotranspiration.

title: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
code: calculate_potential_npp(   x,   method = c("lpjml", "miami", "nceas", "rosenzweig"),   lpjml = list() )

title: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
text: x tibble. climate methods require temp_c (mean annual temperature, degrees C), water_input_mm (precipitation plus irrigation) aet_mm (actual evapotranspiration). lpjml method instead needs spatial key join gridded grass NPP (see lpjml). method Potential-NPP method, one "lpjml" (default), "miami", "nceas" "rosenzweig". lpjml named list options lpjml method (passed LPJmL grass reader). Ignored climate methods.

title: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
text: input tibble npp_potential_dm_t_ha (potential NPP, t DM per hectare) method_npp_potential (method used).

title: Estimate potential net primary production. — calculate_potential_npp
code: calculate_potential_npp(   tibble::tibble(temp_c = 15, water_input_mm = 800, aet_mm = 700),   method = "miami" ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   temp_c water_input_mm aet_mm npp_potential_dm_t_ha method_npp_potential #>    <dbl>          <dbl>  <dbl>                 <dbl> <chr>                #> 1     15            800    700                  12.4 miami

what: Estimate the destinies of crop residues. — calculate_residue_destinies
title: Estimate the destinies of crop residues. — calculate_residue_destinies
text: Splits crop residue dry matter three destinies sum total residue: fed livestock, burned / removed fuel, left field soil incorporation.

title: Estimate the destinies of crop residues. — calculate_residue_destinies

title: Estimate the destinies of crop residues. — calculate_residue_destinies
text: x tibble item_prod_code residue_dm_t. krausmann_regional method also needs region_krausmann (recovery rate) region_hanpp (feed-use fraction). region_krausmann can use recovery-table labels matching regions_full labels. shares method needs year. method Destiny method: "krausmann_regional" (default, Krausmann recovery x HANPP-regional feed-use fraction) "shares" (Spain-specific per-crop-year use/burn shares, flagged to_be_revised).

title: Estimate the destinies of crop residues. — calculate_residue_destinies
text: input tibble residue_feed_dm_t, residue_burn_dm_t, residue_soil_dm_t method_residue_destiny.

what: Calculate uncertainty bounds for livestock emissions. — calculate_uncertainty_bounds
title: Calculate uncertainty bounds for livestock emissions. — calculate_uncertainty_bounds
text: Applies IPCC uncertainty ranges emission estimates. Multipliers sourced uncertainty_ranges table (hardcoded values).

title: Calculate uncertainty bounds for livestock emissions. — calculate_uncertainty_bounds
code: calculate_uncertainty_bounds(data)

what: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
title: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
text: Verify master input-output accounting identity footprint: environmental pressure embodied across final demand traced back origin sector equal direct extension (source pressure) sector. footprint engine zeroes negative coefficients, caps column sums, drops near-zero-output sectors (FABIO conventions), identity holds approximately. check quantifies discrepancy per origin sector instead asserting exact equality. Crucially detects -tracing (pressure silently disappears never reaches final demand), compute_footprint()'s conserve_extensions bounding never reports rescales results downward. positive side extensions traced, matching engine, traces pmax(extensions, 0) ignores sectors output <= output_tol.

title: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
code: check_footprint_conservation(   footprint,   extensions,   labels,   x_vec,   output_tol = 1e-08,   tol = 0.01 )

title: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
text: footprint Footprint tibble compute_footprint(), origin_area, origin_item value columns. extensions Numeric vector environmental extensions per sector, passed compute_footprint(). labels Tibble area_code item_cbs_code mapping sector meaning, passed compute_footprint(). x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. output_tol Minimum output sector traceable. Sectors x_vec <= output_tol contribute zero direct pressure, matching compute_footprint(). tol Relative tolerance conservation status. Discrepancies within tol direct pressure "ok".

title: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
text: tibble one row per origin sector: origin_area: Country pressure occurs. origin_item: Item causing pressure. direct: Direct (source) extension sector. embodied: Footprint traced back sector. discrepancy: embodied - direct. rel_discrepancy: discrepancy / direct (NA direct zero). status: One "ok", "under_traced", "dropped" (embodied zero direct positive) "over_traced". Rows ordered descending absolute relative discrepancy.

title: Check footprint conservation against direct extensions. — check_footprint_conservation
code: z_mat <- matrix(c(0, 5, 10, 0), nrow = 2) x_vec <- c(100, 200) y_mat <- matrix(c(85, 195), ncol = 1) extensions <- c(50, 30) labels <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 1L),   item_cbs_code = c(1L, 2L) ) fp <- compute_footprint(   x_vec = x_vec, y_mat = y_mat, extensions = extensions,   labels = labels, z_mat = z_mat ) #> ℹ Computing footprint for 2 sectors. #>   2 sectors have non-zero extensions. #>   Final demand: 1 column. #> Sparse solve path (no dense Leontief inverse). #> Computing footprints... #> ✔ Footprint complete: 2 non-zero flows. check_footprint_conservation(fp, extensions, labels, x_vec) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   origin_area origin_item direct embodied discrepancy rel_discrepancy status     #>         <int>       <int>  <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl>           <dbl> <chr>      #> 1           1           1     50     47.5     -2.51          -0.0501  under_tra… #> 2           1           2     30     30.0     -0.0376        -0.00125 ok

title: Check the commodity balance sheet supply-use identity. — check_supply_use_balance

title: Check the commodity balance sheet supply-use identity. — check_supply_use_balance
text: cbs Wide commodity balance sheet, e.g. get_wide_cbs(). tol Absolute tolerance (data's mass units) row count balanced.

title: Check the commodity balance sheet supply-use identity. — check_supply_use_balance
text: tibble key columns present cbs plus: supply: Total supply. use: Total use. abs_diff: abs(supply - use). rel_diff: abs_diff / supply (NA supply zero). balanced: TRUE abs_diff <= tol. Rows ordered descending absolute difference.

title: Compute environmental footprints. — compute_footprint
text: Trace environmental extensions supply chain using Leontief inverse, following FABIO methodology (Bruckner et al., 2019). footprint shows much environmental pressure (e.g. land use, water, emissions) embodied final consumption product country. multiplier matrix computed \(MP_{ij} = (e_i / X_i) \cdot L_{ij}\), \(e_i\) extension sector \(\). demand category, footprint decomposed per target item using FABIO diagonal approach: \(FP = MP \cdot \text{diag}(y)\), aggregated item. large systems, pass z_mat x_vec instead l_inv. solves \((- ) x = Y\) directly using sparse LU factorisation, avoiding dense Leontief inverse entirely reducing memory \(O(n^2)\) \(O(nnz)\).

title: Compute environmental footprints. — compute_footprint
code: compute_footprint(   l_inv = NULL,   x_vec,   y_mat,   extensions,   labels,   z_mat = NULL,   fd_labels = NULL,   output_tol = 1e-08,   value_added_floor = 0.001,   max_column_sum = 100,   conserve_extensions = TRUE,   report_conservation = FALSE )

title: Compute environmental footprints. — compute_footprint
text: l_inv Leontief inverse matrix compute_leontief_inverse(). Ignored z_mat provided. x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. y_mat Final demand matrix build_io_model(). extensions Numeric vector environmental extensions (e.g. hectares land use) per sector. Must length x_vec. labels Tibble area_code item_cbs_code mapping row/column indices meaning. build_io_model(). z_mat Optional inter-industry flow matrix build_io_model(). provided, system solved directly (sparse LU), l_inv needed. fd_labels Optional tibble labelling Y columns. Pass fd_labels[[]] build_io_model() output. provided, footprints decomposed per target item using FABIO diagonal approach, result includes target_fd column. omitted, columns Y treated sectors (appropriate Y square). output_tol Minimum output considered valid computing extension intensities. Sectors x_vec <= output_tol get zero intensity avoid infinite numerically explosive footprints zero-output residuals. value_added_floor Minimum share sector's output treated non-intermediate leakage constructing z_mat max_column_sum left low-level default. Ignored precomputed l_inv supplied without z_mat. max_column_sum Maximum allowed column sum using z_mat. Physical biomass systems can require one unit intermediate input per unit output, footprint path defaults 100 clips extreme columns caused residual inconsistencies tiny outputs. conserve_extensions TRUE, rescale positive footprint flows within origin area/item sum exceed corresponding positive extension total. keeps footprint outputs conservative capped coefficients negative final demand columns otherwise make positive-paths larger source extension. report_conservation TRUE, emit message computing footprint reporting conservation gap (share direct extension embodied final demand), via check_footprint_conservation(). default gap opt-never silent requested.

title: Compute environmental footprints. — compute_footprint
text: tibble footprint results containing: origin_area: Country pressure occurs. origin_polity_code: WHEP polity origin_area. origin_item: Item causing pressure. target_area: Country consuming product. target_polity_code: WHEP polity target_area. target_item: Item consumed. target_fd: Demand category (e.g. "food"). present fd_labels provided. value: Footprint value extension units.

title: Compute environmental footprints. — compute_footprint
code: z_mat <- matrix(c(0, 5, 10, 0), nrow = 2) x_vec <- c(100, 200) l_inv <- compute_leontief_inverse(z_mat, x_vec) #> Computing Leontief inverse (2x2 matrix)... #> Inverting (I - A)... #> ✔ Leontief inverse computed. y_mat <- matrix(c(85, 195), ncol = 1) extensions <- c(50, 30) labels <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 1L),   item_cbs_code = c(1L, 2L) )  # Small system: pass pre-computed L compute_footprint(l_inv, x_vec, y_mat, extensions, labels) #> ℹ Computing footprint for 2 sectors. #>   2 sectors have non-zero extensions. #>   Final demand: 1 column. #> Computing multiplier matrix... #> Computing footprints... #> ✔ Footprint complete: 2 non-zero flows. #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>   origin_area origin_polity_code origin_polity_name origin_polity_has_geometry #>         <int> <chr>              <chr>              <lgl>                      #> 1           1 ARM-1991-2025      Armenia            TRUE                       #> 2           1 ARM-1991-2025      Armenia            TRUE                       #> # ℹ 7 more variables: origin_item <int>, target_area <int>, #> #   target_polity_code <chr>, target_polity_name <chr>, #> #   target_polity_has_geometry <lgl>, target_item <int>, value <dbl>  # Using Z directly (computes L internally) compute_footprint(   x_vec = x_vec, y_mat = y_mat,   extensions = extensions, labels = labels,   z_mat = z_mat ) #> ℹ Computing footprint for 2 sectors. #>   2 sectors have non-zero extensions. #>   Final demand: 1 column. #> Sparse solve path (no dense Leontief inverse). #> Computing footprints... #> ✔ Footprint complete: 2 non-zero flows. #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>   origin_area origin_polity_code origin_polity_name origin_polity_has_geometry #>         <int> <chr>              <chr>              <lgl>                      #> 1           1 ARM-1991-2025      Armenia            TRUE                       #> 2           1 ARM-1991-2025      Armenia            TRUE                       #> # ℹ 7 more variables: origin_item <int>, target_area <int>, #> #   target_polity_code <chr>, target_polity_name <chr>, #> #   target_polity_has_geometry <lgl>, target_item <int>, value <dbl>

what: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
title: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
text: Estimate consumption-based land footprints propagating direct land use bilateral physical trade network, approach independent Leontief inverse used compute_footprint(). country's supply pool (production plus imports) carries embodied-land intensity s; solving balance \((D - M) s = L\) per item gives s, D diagonal supply throughput, M routes import intensities, L direct land. footprint consumption (supply minus exports) consumption * s. Unlike multi-regional input-output method, tracer handles trade dimension, inter-product processing transformations. Comparing two compare_footprint_methods() isolates effect assumption – apples--apples stress test shared FAOSTAT data.

title: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
code: compute_footprint_balance(production, trade, extension)

title: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
text: production Tibble area_code, item_cbs_code value (quantity produced). trade Tibble from_code, to_code, item_cbs_code value (quantity exported from_code to_code). extension Tibble area_code, item_cbs_code value (direct land use domestic production).

title: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
text: tibble area_code (consuming country), item_cbs_code, value (embodied land consumption) method ("land_balance").

title: Compute land footprints by physical trade balance. — compute_footprint_balance
code: production <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 2L),   item_cbs_code = c(10L, 10L),   value = c(100, 0) ) trade <- tibble::tibble(   from_code = 1L, to_code = 2L, item_cbs_code = 10L, value = 40 ) extension <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 2L),   item_cbs_code = c(10L, 10L),   value = c(50, 0) ) compute_footprint_balance(production, trade, extension) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   area_code item_cbs_code value method       #>       <int>         <int> <dbl> <chr>        #> 1         1            10    30 land_balance #> 2         2            10    20 land_balance

title: Compute first-use footprint paths. — compute_footprint_paths
text: Decompose origin footprint first sector directly uses origin product footprint reaches final demand. useful Sankey views show paths origin product -> first-use area -> first-use product -> final-demand area. decomposition uses IO identity \(x = d + x\). selected origin sector \(\) final-demand target, origin requirement \(x_i\) split direct final demand \(d_i\) direct intermediate use \(A_{ij} x_j\). Values multiplied origin extension intensity \(e_i / X_i\).

title: Compute first-use footprint paths. — compute_footprint_paths
code: compute_footprint_paths(   z_mat,   x_vec,   y_mat,   extensions,   labels,   fd_labels,   origin_area = NULL,   origin_item = NULL,   output_tol = 1e-08,   value_added_floor = 0.001,   conserve_extensions = TRUE,   min_value = 0 )

title: Compute first-use footprint paths. — compute_footprint_paths
text: z_mat Inter-industry flow matrix build_io_model(). x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. y_mat Final demand matrix build_io_model(). extensions Numeric vector environmental extensions per sector. labels Tibble area_code item_cbs_code mapping sectors. fd_labels Tibble labelling Y columns, build_io_model(). origin_area Optional area code vector limiting origin sectors. origin_item Optional item code vector limiting origin sectors. output_tol Minimum output considered valid computing extension intensities. value_added_floor Minimum non-intermediate leakage share used constructing technical coefficients z_mat. conserve_extensions TRUE, rescale positive paths within origin area/item sum exceed corresponding positive extension total. min_value Drop paths values less equal value returning.

title: Compute first-use footprint paths. — compute_footprint_paths
text: tibble origin_area, origin_item, use_area, use_item, target_area, target_item, role-specific polity metadata, target_fd, path_type, value.

text: Decompose origin footprint area item product supplied final demand. adds missing FABIO-viewer style phase origin product final-demand area: origin product -> supplied product area -> supplied product -> final-demand area. Unlike compute_footprint_paths(), show first direct intermediate input. shows downstream product row Y whose final demand carries origin footprint.

title: Compute final-product footprint paths. — compute_fp_product_paths
code: compute_fp_product_paths(   z_mat,   x_vec,   y_mat,   extensions,   labels,   fd_labels,   origin_area = NULL,   origin_item = NULL,   output_tol = 1e-08,   value_added_floor = 0.001,   conserve_extensions = TRUE,   min_value = 0 )

title: Compute final-product footprint paths. — compute_fp_product_paths
text: z_mat Inter-industry flow matrix build_io_model(). x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. y_mat Final demand matrix build_io_model(). extensions Numeric vector environmental extensions per sector. labels Tibble area_code item_cbs_code mapping sectors. fd_labels Tibble labelling Y columns, build_io_model(). origin_area Optional area code vector limiting origin sectors. origin_item Optional item code vector limiting origin sectors. output_tol Minimum output considered valid computing extension intensities. value_added_floor Minimum non-intermediate leakage share used constructing technical coefficients z_mat. conserve_extensions TRUE, rescale positive paths within origin area/item sum exceed corresponding positive extension total. min_value Drop paths values less equal value returning.

title: Compute final-product footprint paths. — compute_fp_product_paths
text: tibble origin_area, origin_item, product_area, product_item, target_area, role-specific polity metadata, target_fd, value.

title: Compute Leontief inverse. — compute_leontief_inverse
text: Compute Leontief inverse matrix intermediate flows total output. Leontief inverse captures direct indirect requirements across entire supply chain, enabling footprint tracing. technical coefficients matrix computed \(A_{ij} = Z_{ij} / X_j\), representing input sector \(\) needed per unit output sector \(j\). Column sums capped using max_column_sum avoid singular systems inconsistent supply-use data. default uses 1 - value_added_floor, preserving previous conservative behavior explicit Leontief inverses. Leontief inverse \(L = (- )^{-1}\). large systems (thousands sectors) function usable: dense L matrix requires \(n^2 \times 8\) bytes memory (e.g. ~4.8 GiB n = 25 000). Use compute_footprint() directly z_mat x_vec instead, solves \((- ) x = Y\) without ever materialising L. Accepts dense sparse (Matrix package) inputs.

title: Compute Leontief inverse. — compute_leontief_inverse
code: compute_leontief_inverse(   z_mat,   x_vec,   max_n = 5000,   value_added_floor = 0.001,   max_column_sum = 1 - value_added_floor )

title: Compute Leontief inverse. — compute_leontief_inverse
text: z_mat Square numeric matrix inter-industry flows. Entry \(Z_{ij}\) flow sector \(\) sector \(j\). Can dense sparse. x_vec Numeric vector total output per sector. Must length nrow(z_mat). max_n Maximum system size aborting. Defaults 5000. Set higher risk memory exhaustion. value_added_floor Minimum share sector's output treated non-intermediate leakage constructing max_column_sum left default. max_column_sum Maximum allowed column sum . Columns value rescaled. Defaults 1 - value_added_floor.

title: Compute Leontief inverse. — compute_leontief_inverse
text: Leontief inverse matrix \(L\). Negative values set zero. Returns dense matrix.

title: Compute Leontief inverse. — compute_leontief_inverse
code: z_mat <- matrix(c(0, 5, 10, 0), nrow = 2) x_vec <- c(100, 200) compute_leontief_inverse(z_mat, x_vec) #> Computing Leontief inverse (2x2 matrix)... #> Inverting (I - A)... #> ✔ Leontief inverse computed. #>            [,1]       [,2] #> [1,] 1.00250627 0.05012531 #> [2,] 0.05012531 1.00250627

text: Provides N flows Spanish agro-food system national level 1860 2020. dataset national equivalent provincial GRAFS model represents Spain single system without internal trade provinces. production, consumption soil inputs aggregated nationally calculating trade outside.

title: GRAFS Nitrogen (N) flows — create_n_prov_destiny
text: Provides N flows spanish agro-food system provincial level 1860 2020. dataset base GRAFS model contains data megagrams N (MgN) year, province, item, origin destiny. Thereby, origin column represents N comes , includes N soil inputs, imports production. destiny column shows N goes , includes export, population food, population uses feed cropland (case N soil inputs). Processed items, residues, woody crops, grazed weeds taken account.

what: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand
title: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand
text: Calculate gross energy (GE) intake per IPCC 2019 Tier 2 equations (Vol 4, Ch 10). Estimates net energy components maintenance, activity, lactation, work, pregnancy, growth, wool, derives total gross energy using REM/REG ratio approach IPCC Eq 10.16. coefficients come internal package data.

title: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand
code: estimate_energy_demand(data, method = "ipcc2019")

title: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand
text: data dataframe columns species, cohort, heads, optionally iso3. Optional production columns: weight, milk_yield_kg_day, fat_percent, weight_gain_kg_day, work_hours_day, pregnant_fraction, temperature_c, diet_quality, grazing_distance_km, system. method Method calculation (default "ipcc2019").

title: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand
text: Dataframe added gross_energy (MJ/day), intermediate net energy components, method_energy tracking column.

title: Estimate energy demand (Gross Energy) - Tier 2 — estimate_energy_demand

what: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
title: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
text: Converts realised feed intake (output redistribute_feed()) excreted nitrogen, carbon volatile solids per year x territory x sub_territory x livestock_category. excretion methods share one canonical nitrogen intake, n_intake = sum(intake_dm_t * feed_n_content), methods directly comparable.

title: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
code: estimate_n_excretion(intake, options = list())

title: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
text: intake tibble realised feed intake least year, territory, sub_territory, livestock_category, item_cbs_code, feed_quality intake_dm_t (redistribute_feed() result). options named list method options: method: "intake_minus_retention" (default, n_intake * (1 - n_retention_frac)) "intake_minus_product_n" (n_intake - product_n). method_vs: "intake_digestibility" (default, intake_dm_t * (1 - digestibility) * (1 - ash)). product_n: tibble (year, territory, sub_territory, livestock_category, product_n) required "intake_minus_product_n".

title: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
text: tibble one row per year x territory x sub_territory x livestock_category columns n_intake, n_excretion, c_excretion, vs_excretion, method_n_excretion method_vs.

title: Estimate livestock nitrogen, carbon and volatile-solids excretion. — estimate_n_excretion
code: intake <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~territory, ~sub_territory, ~livestock_category,   ~item_cbs_code, ~feed_quality, ~intake_dm_t,   2020L, "ES", NA, "Cattle_milk", 2513L, "high_quality", 100,   2020L, "ES", NA, "Cattle_milk", NA, "grass", 500 ) estimate_n_excretion(intake) #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>    year territory sub_territory livestock_category n_intake n_excretion #>   <int> <chr>     <lgl>         <chr>                 <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1  2020 ES        NA            Cattle_milk            11.9        9.49 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: c_excretion <dbl>, vs_excretion <dbl>, #> #   method_n_excretion <chr>, method_vs <chr>

what: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
title: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
text: Fills gaps (NA values) time-dependent variable linear interpolation two points, carrying forward backwards last initial values, respectively. also creates new variable indicating source filled values.

title: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
code: fill_linear(   data,   value_col,   time_col = year,   interpolate = TRUE,   fill_forward = TRUE,   fill_backward = TRUE,   value_smooth_window = NULL,   .by = NULL,   .copy = TRUE )

title: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
text: data data frame containing one observation per row. value_col column containing gaps filled. time_col column containing time values. Default: year. interpolate Logical. TRUE (default), performs linear interpolation. fill_forward Logical. TRUE (default), carries last value forward. fill_backward Logical. TRUE (default), carries first value backward. value_smooth_window integer specifying window size centered moving average applied variable gap-filling. Useful variables high inter-annual variability. NULL (default), smoothing applied. .character vector grouping variables (optional). .copy Logical. TRUE (default), data.table inputs defensively copied mutation. Set FALSE caller owns data need original preserved.

title: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
text: tibble data frame (ungrouped) gaps value_col filled, new "source" variable created indicating value original , case estimated, gapfilling method used.

title: Fill gaps by linear interpolation, or carrying forward or backward. — fill_linear
code: sample_tibble <- tibble::tibble(   category = c("a", "a", "a", "a", "a", "a", "b", "b", "b", "b", "b", "b"),   year = c(     "2015", "2016", "2017", "2018", "2019", "2020",     "2015", "2016", "2017", "2018", "2019", "2020"   ),   value = c(NA, 3, NA, NA, 0, NA, 1, NA, NA, NA, 5, NA), ) fill_linear(sample_tibble, value, .by = c("category")) #> # A tibble: 12 × 4 #>    category year  value source_value                  #>    <chr>    <chr> <dbl> <chr>                         #>  1 a        2015      3 First value carried backwards #>  2 a        2016      3 Original                      #>  3 a        2017      2 Linear interpolation          #>  4 a        2018      1 Linear interpolation          #>  5 a        2019      0 Original                      #>  6 a        2020      0 Last value carried forward    #>  7 b        2015      1 Original                      #>  8 b        2016      2 Linear interpolation          #>  9 b        2017      3 Linear interpolation          #> 10 b        2018      4 Linear interpolation          #> 11 b        2019      5 Original                      #> 12 b        2020      5 Last value carried forward    fill_linear(   sample_tibble,   value,   interpolate = FALSE,   .by = c("category"), ) #> # A tibble: 12 × 4 #>    category year  value source_value                  #>    <chr>    <chr> <dbl> <chr>                         #>  1 a        2015      3 First value carried backwards #>  2 a        2016      3 Original                      #>  3 a        2017     NA Gap not filled                #>  4 a        2018     NA Gap not filled                #>  5 a        2019      0 Original                      #>  6 a        2020      0 Last value carried forward    #>  7 b        2015      1 Original                      #>  8 b        2016     NA Gap not filled                #>  9 b        2017     NA Gap not filled                #> 10 b        2018     NA Gap not filled                #> 11 b        2019      5 Original                      #> 12 b        2020      5 Last value carried forward

what: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
text: Fills missing values using growth rates proxy variable (reference series). Supports regional aggregations, weighting, linear interpolation small gaps.

title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
code: fill_proxy_growth(   data,   value_col,   proxy_col,   time_col = year,   .by = NULL,   max_gap = Inf,   max_gap_linear = 3,   fill_scope = NULL,   value_smooth_window = NULL,   proxy_smooth_window = 1,   output_format = "clean",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
text: data data frame containing time series data. value_col column containing values fill. proxy_col Character vector. Proxy variable(s) calculating growth rates. Supports multiple syntax formats: Simple numeric proxy (e.g., "population"): Auto-detects numeric columns uses proxy variable. Inherits .parameter compute proxy values per group. Simple categorical proxy (e.g., "region"): Auto-detects categorical columns interprets value_col:region. Aggregates value_col specified groups. Advanced syntax (e.g., "gdp:region"): Format "variable:group1+group2". Aggregates variable specified groups. Hierarchical fallback (e.g., c("population", "gdp:region")): Tries first proxy, falls back second first fails. Weighted aggregation (e.g., "gdp[population]"): Weight variable specified column aggregation. time_col column containing time values. Default: year. .character vector grouping variables (optional). max_gap Numeric. Maximum gap size fill using growth method. Default: Inf. max_gap_linear Numeric. Maximum gap size linear interpolation fallback. Default: 3. fill_scope Quosure. Filter expression limit filling scope. Default: NULL. value_smooth_window Integer. Window size centered moving average applied value column gap-filling. Useful variables high inter-annual variability. NULL (default), smoothing applied. proxy_smooth_window Integer. Window size moving average smoothing proxy reference values computing growth rates. Default: 1. output_format Character. Output format: "clean" "detailed". Default: "clean". verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
text: data frame filled values. output_format = "clean", returns original columns updated value_col added source column. "detailed", includes intermediate columns.

title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
text: Combined Growth Sequence (Hierarchical Interpolation): using multiple proxies hierarchical fallback, function implements intelligent combined growth sequence strategy: Better proxies (earlier hierarchy) tried first gap. better proxy partial coverage within gap, growth rates used covered positions. Fallback proxies fill remaining positions better proxies available. Values filled better proxies protected overwritten.

title: Fill gaps using growth rates from proxy variables — fill_proxy_growth
code: # Fill GDP using population as proxy data <- tibble::tibble(   country = rep("ESP", 4),   year = 2010:2013,   gdp = c(1000, NA, NA, 1200),   population = c(46, 46.5, 47, 47.5) )  fill_proxy_growth(   data,   value_col = gdp,   proxy_col = "population",   .by = "country" ) #> Auto-detected 'population' as numeric -> using as source variable #> Calculating growth rates for: population #> Step 2: Applying hierarchical filling... #>   Applying proxy level 1: population #> Total filled: 2 out of 2 missing values #>   year country      gdp population    source_gdp #> 1 2010     ESP 1000.000       46.0      original #> 2 2011     ESP 1066.667       46.5 linear_interp #> 3 2012     ESP 1133.333       47.0 linear_interp #> 4 2013     ESP 1200.000       47.5      original

title: Livestock feed intake — get_feed_intake
text: example TRUE, return small example output without downloading remote data. Default FALSE. grain Spatial grain feed allocation. "national" (default, one allocation per country) "local" (per-cell 0.5-degree engine, heavy run via build_feed_intake_local(); calling redirects ). demand_tier Demand-estimation tier. "ipcc" (default, rigorous IPCC Tier-2 energy demand ruminant species covers, Bouwman FCR pigs poultry, Krausmann per-head draft / species) "fcr" (Bouwman / Krausmann feed-conversion magnitude every species). grains allocate redistribute_feed(). feed_mode Whether distribute surplus feed availability. "historical" (default) suppresses surplus-distribution pass: CBS feed element treated realised consumption, leftover availability dumped onto variable-demand livestock (inflate non-grass intake). "scenario" distributes surplus. years Integer vector years build, NULL (default) every year production data (1850-2023 via LUH2 extension). Restricting range cuts run time proportionally; allocation independent per year, subset returns exactly rows years.

title: Livestock feed intake — get_feed_intake

Single Scavenging item. supply: computed amount tonnes item fed animal, sharing total item feed use Commodity Balance Sheet among livestock. intake: actual amount tonnes animal needs, can less theoretical used amount supply. intake_dry_matter: amount specified intake considering dry matter, less intake. loss: amount used feed. supply - intake. loss_share: percent lost. loss / supply.

title: Commodity balance sheet data. — get_wide_cbs
text: Retrieve supply use parts commodity balance sheet (CBS) item. Stock variations split two non-negative columns following FABIO methodology.

title: Commodity balance sheet data. — get_wide_cbs
code: get_wide_cbs(example = FALSE)

title: Commodity balance sheet data. — get_wide_cbs
text: example TRUE, return small example output without downloading remote data. Default FALSE.

title: Commodity balance sheet data. — get_wide_cbs
text: tibble commodity balance sheet data wide format. contains following columns: year: year recorded event occurred. area_code: code country data . code details see e.g. add_area_name(). item_cbs_code: FAOSTAT internal code item. code details see e.g. add_item_cbs_name(). columns quantities total supply total use balanced. Units tonnes items, heads live animals (see items_cbs item_type). supply: production: Produced locally. import: Obtained importing countries. stock_withdrawal: Biomass taken storage (non-negative). Positive stocks decrease. use: food: Food humans. feed: Food animals. export: Released export countries. seed: Intended new production. processing: Used obtain subproducts. other_uses: use included . stock_addition: Biomass placed storage (non-negative). Positive stocks increase. additional column domestic_supply computed total use excluding export.

what: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
title: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
text: Compute per-(area, item) weight distributing reported fallow crops, gridded crop placement crop x agro-climatic-zone propensity. grid cell, rainfed crop area multiplied crop's propensity cell's agro-climatic zone (derived GAEZ length growing period thermal climate), summed country x item. Dryland cereals/pulses score high arid/semi-arid zones, rainfed rice high humid tropics (rice-fallow); perennials irrigated share score ~zero.

title: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
code: gridded_fallow_weights(gridded_crops, grid_aez = NULL, propensity = NULL)

title: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
text: gridded_crops Tibble keyed grid cell item_cbs_code columns lon, lat, area_code, rainfed_ha. grid_aez Tibble lon, lat, lgp (length growing period days), thermal (GAEZ thermal-climate class). NULL, packaged grid_aez.csv used. propensity Tibble item_cbs_code, zone, fallow_propensity. NULL, packaged fallow_propensity.csv used.

title: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
text: tibble area_code, item_cbs_code, weight.

title: Build agro-climatic, rainfed-gated fallow allocation weights. — gridded_fallow_weights
code: gridded_crops <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon, ~lat, ~area_code, ~item_cbs_code, ~rainfed_ha,   0.25, 50.25, 1L, 2511L, 500 ) grid_aez <- tibble::tribble(~lon, ~lat, ~lgp, ~thermal, 0.25, 50.25, 100, 7L) propensity <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_cbs_code, ~zone, ~fallow_propensity,   2511L, "semiarid", 0.8 ) gridded_fallow_weights(gridded_crops, grid_aez, propensity) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   area_code item_cbs_code weight #>       <int>         <int>  <dbl> #> 1         1          2511    400

what: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
title: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
text: Harmonize data containing "simple" "1:n" mappings. "simple" covers 1:1 N:1 relationships (values summed). "1:n" groups (one original item splits several harmonized items) function computes value shares across full year range, interpolates missing shares, applies split values. Important 1:n mappings: original item splits multiple harmonized items (e.g., "wheatrice" "wheat" "rice"), provide one row per target item_code_harm. row item, year, value, differing item_code_harm. example, disaggregate "wheatrice": Row 1: item = "wheatrice", item_code_harm = 1 Row 2: item = "wheatrice", item_code_harm = 2 provide single row; function create duplicates automatically.

title: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
code: harmonize_interpolate(data, ...)

title: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
text: data data frame containing least columns: item: String, original item name. item_code_harm: Numeric, code harmonized item. year: Numeric, year observation. value: Numeric, value observation. type: String, "simple" "1:n". ... Additional grouping columns provided bare names.

title: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
text: tibble columns: item_code: Numeric, code harmonized item. year: Numeric, year observation. value: Numeric, summed value observation. additional grouping columns.

title: Harmonize advanced cases with interpolation for 1:N groups — harmonize_interpolate
code: # Simple-only data (no 1:n rows) df_simple <- tibble::tribble(   ~item, ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   "wheat", 1, 2000, 5, "simple",   "barley", 2, 2000, 3, "simple",   "oats", 2, 2000, 2, "simple" ) harmonize_interpolate(df_simple) #> ℹ Only simple harmonization detected, returning simple harmonizations only. #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   item_code  year value #>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1         1  2000     5 #> 2         2  2000     5  # Mixed simple + 1:n data df_mixed <- tibble::tribble(   ~item, ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   "wheatrice", 1, 2000, 20, "1:n",   "wheatrice", 2, 2000, 20, "1:n",   "wheat", 1, 2000, 8, "simple",   "rice", 2, 2000, 12, "simple" ) harmonize_interpolate(df_mixed) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   item_code  year value #>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1         1  2000    16 #> 2         2  2000    24  # Multiple years with share interpolation # Shares are known in 2000 and 2002; 2001 is interpolated. df_years <- tibble::tribble(   ~item, ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   "wheat", 1, 2000, 6, "simple",   "rice", 2, 2000, 4, "simple",   "wheatrice", 1, 2001, 10, "1:n",   "wheatrice", 2, 2001, 10, "1:n",   "wheat", 1, 2002, 8, "simple",   "rice", 2, 2002, 2, "simple" ) harmonize_interpolate(df_years) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   item_code  year value #>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1         1  2001     7 #> 2         2  2001     3 #> 3         1  2000     6 #> 4         2  2000     4 #> 5         1  2002     8 #> 6         2  2002     2  # With extra grouping columns df_grouped <- tibble::tribble(   ~item, ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type, ~country,   "wheat", 1, 2000, 6, "simple", "usa",   "rice", 2, 2000, 4, "simple", "usa",   "wheatrice", 1, 2001, 10, "1:n", "usa",   "wheatrice", 2, 2001, 10, "1:n", "usa",   "wheat", 1, 2002, 8, "simple", "usa",   "rice", 2, 2002, 2, "simple", "usa",   "wheat", 1, 2002, 8, "simple", "germany" ) harmonize_interpolate(df_grouped, country) #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   item_code  year country value #>       <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   <dbl> #> 1         1  2001 usa         7 #> 2         2  2001 usa         3 #> 3         1  2000 usa         6 #> 4         2  2000 usa         4 #> 5         1  2002 usa         8 #> 6         2  2002 usa         2 #> 7         1  2002 germany     8

title: Harmonize rows labeled
text: Sum value rows type == "simple". covers 1:1 N:1 item mappings, since cases values simply summed. results grouped item_code_harm, year additional grouping columns supplied via ....

title: Harmonize rows labeled
code: harmonize_simple(data, ...)

title: Harmonize rows labeled
text: data data frame containing least columns: item_code_harm: Numeric, code harmonized item. year: Numeric, year observation. value: Numeric, value observation. type: String, harmonization type. "simple" rows used. ... Additional grouping columns supplied bare names.

title: Harmonize rows labeled
text: tibble columns: item_code_harm: Numeric, code harmonized item. year: Numeric, year observation. value: Numeric, summed value observation. additional grouping columns.

title: Harmonize rows labeled
code: # 1:1 mapping: one original item -> one harmonized code df_one_to_one <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   1, 2000, 10, "simple",   2, 2000, 3, "simple",   1, 2001, 12, "simple",   2, 2001, 5, "simple" ) harmonize_simple(df_one_to_one) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   item_code_harm  year value #>            <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1              1  2000    10 #> 2              2  2000     3 #> 3              1  2001    12 #> 4              2  2001     5  # N:1 mapping: multiple items map to the same code df_many_to_one <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   1, 2000, 4, "simple",   1, 2000, 6, "simple",   2, 2000, 3, "simple" ) harmonize_simple(df_many_to_one) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   item_code_harm  year value #>            <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1              1  2000    10 #> 2              2  2000     3  # With an extra grouping column (e.g. country) df_grouped <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type, ~country,   1, 2000, 4, "simple", "usa",   1, 2000, 6, "simple", "usa",   1, 2000, 9, "simple", "germany",   2, 2000, 3, "simple", "usa" ) harmonize_simple(df_grouped, country) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   item_code_harm  year country value #>            <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   <dbl> #> 1              1  2000 usa        10 #> 2              1  2000 germany     9 #> 3              2  2000 usa         3  # Rows with type != "simple" are ignored df_mixed <- tibble::tribble(   ~item_code_harm, ~year, ~value, ~type,   1, 2000, 10, "simple",   1, 2000, 99, "1:n",   2, 2000, 3, "simple" ) harmonize_simple(df_mixed) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   item_code_harm  year value #>            <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1              1  2000    10 #> 2              2  2000     3

title: Full CBS item table — items_full
code: items_full

title: Full CBS item table — items_full
text: tibble row corresponds one CBS item. contains following columns: item_cbs: Name CBS item. item_cbs_code: Numeric CBS item code. comm_code: Commodity code used process-based modelling (may contain "#N/" applicable). proc_code: Process code (may contain "#N/" applicable). proc: Process name (may contain "#N/" applicable). unit: Measurement unit (typically "tonnes"). group: Broad item group. Common values include "Additives", "Crop products", "Crop residues", "Draught", "Fish", "Forestry", "Grass", "Livestock", others. feedtype_graniv: Feed type classification granivores (e.g., "additives", "concentrates", "roughages"). feedtype_grazers: Feed type classification grazers. comm_group: Sub-group commodity (e.g., "Additives", "Alcohol", "Ethanol", "Oil cakes", "processing residues"). Cat_1: Primary category label used material flow accounting. Name_biomass: Corresponding item name biomass_coefs, enabling joins biomass coefficient table. dbMFA_items: Item identifier used material flow analysis database. FEDNA: Item name used FEDNA feed composition tables. default_destiny: Default CBS use category item. One "Feed", "Food", "Other_uses", "Processing", NA.

code: items_prim

title: Primary production items linked to CBS — items_prim
text: tibble row corresponds one production item. contains following columns: item_prod: Name production item (e.g., "Wheat", "Rice"). item_prod_code: FAOSTAT production item code (character). item_cbs: Name corresponding CBS item. item_cbs_code: Numeric CBS item code. Farm_class: Farm system classification. Crop items use codes "COP" (cereals, oilseeds, protein crops), "Vegetables", "Fruits", "Olive", "Grapevine", "Other_crops". Livestock items use "Dairy_cows", "Cattle", "Monogastric", "Sheep_goats", "Bees", "Game". NA non-farm items. Cat_Labour: Labour category used agricultural labour analyses. Cat_FAO1: Top-level FAO commodity category. group: Item group classification. One "Primary crops", "Crop products", "Livestock products", "Grass", "Crop residues", "Scavenging", "Livestock".

title: Full production item table — items_prod_full
code: items_prod_full

title: Full production item table — items_prod_full
text: tibble row corresponds one production item. contains following columns: item_prod: Name production item. item_prod_code: FAOSTAT production item code (character). item_cbs: Name corresponding CBS item. item_cbs_code: Numeric CBS item code. group: Item group. One "Primary crops", "Crop products", "Livestock products", "Crop residues", "Grassland", "Scavenging". live_anim: Name parent live animal livestock-derived items (NA crop items). live_anim_code: Numeric CBS code parent live animal (NA crop items). Cat_Krausmann: Item category used Krausmann et al. biomass flow accounting. Name_biomass: Corresponding item name biomass_coefs, enabling joins biomass coefficients. Name_Eurostat: Corresponding item name Eurostat agricultural statistics. Name: Alternative display name item. Cat_Labour: Labour category used agricultural labour analyses. Cat_FAO1: Top-level FAO commodity category (e.g., "Cereals", "Oilcrops"). Cat_Origin: Origin-based commodity category. One "Cereals", "Vegetables Fruits", "Sugar Stimulants", "Oil Crops", "Fodder crops", "Fibres Crude Materials", NA. Cat_Use: Use-based commodity category (e.g., "Grains", "Oils Fats", "Fodder crops", "Beverages, sugar stimulants"). Order: Numeric ordering field used sorting items consistently across outputs. Categ: General category label used analyses. Farm_class: Farm system classification (see items_prim values). c3_c4: Photosynthetic pathway. One "c3", "c4", NA. ann_per_nfx: Annual/perennial nitrogen fixation trait. One "ann" (annual), "per" (perennial), "nfx" (nitrogen-fixing), NA. Cat_1: Primary category label material flow accounting. Cat_2: Secondary category label. Cat_3: Tertiary category label. Cat_4: Quaternary category label. Herb_Woody: Plant growth form. One "Herbaceous", "Woody", NA. Crop_irrig: Irrigation category used water-use analyses. Cat_Org: Organic farming category classification. Cat_Ymax: Maximum attainable yield category. Cat_Ymax_leg: Legend label Cat_Ymax category.

what: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
title: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
text: Creates function(centroids_sf, year) suitable APIs accept spatial covariate density, build_constant_territory_series(). covariate built prepared WHEP/LPJmL spatialization inputs, either local input_dir pinned WHEP spatialization inputs input_dir = NULL.

title: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
code: make_lpjml_covariate(   input_dir = NULL,   years = NULL,   weighting = c("total_cropland", "crop_pattern"),   item_prod_code = NULL,   cft_mapping = whep::cft_mapping )

title: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
text: input_dir Directory holding prepared WHEP/LPJmL input parquets. NULL, function reads pinned spatialization inputs via whep_read_file(). years Integer vector years retain. NULL, years present required input used. weighting Either "total_cropland", "crop_pattern", custom function(centroids_sf, year). custom function returned unchanged. item_prod_code FAOSTAT/WHEP production item code. Required weighting = "crop_pattern". cft_mapping Mapping WHEP item codes CFT/LUH2 types. Defaults cft_mapping.

title: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
text: function function(centroids_sf, year) returning non-negative density values aligned centroids_sf.

title: Build an LPJmL/WHEP spatial covariate function — make_lpjml_covariate
code: # A custom covariate function is returned unchanged, ready to plug into # build_constant_territory_series(). Here a uniform (area-weighting) density: uniform <- function(centroids_sf, year) rep(1, nrow(centroids_sf)) covariate <- make_lpjml_covariate(weighting = uniform) identical(covariate, uniform) #> [1] TRUE  # The "total_cropland" and "crop_pattern" modes instead read prepared # WHEP/LPJmL spatialization parquets from `input_dir` (or pinned inputs).
