title: Interactive footprint Sankey viewer — plot_footprint_sankey
text: Create browser-based Sankey viewer footprint table, output compute_footprint(). viewer includes stage filters, per-stage node limit controls, minimum-flow threshold, hover tooltips, click--highlight interactions, SVG download. row treated one path selected stages, value_col giving path size. Rows summed plotting, repeated paths shown one flow. default stages origin_area, origin_item, target_item, target_area; target_fd appended automatically present. columns origin_area_name target_item_name present, used display labels.

title: Interactive footprint Sankey viewer — plot_footprint_sankey
code: plot_footprint_sankey(   footprints,   stages = NULL,   value_col = "value",   label_cols = NULL,   max_nodes = 10,   other_label = "Other",   stage_max_nodes = NULL,   stage_other_labels = NULL,   embed_max_nodes = Inf,   stage_embed_max_nodes = NULL,   min_share = 0,   title = "Footprint Sankey Viewer",   subtitle = NULL,   value_label = "footprint",   width = "100%",   height = 680,   file = NULL,   open = FALSE )

title: Interactive footprint Sankey viewer — plot_footprint_sankey
text: footprints data frame footprint paths numeric value column. stages Character vector columns use Sankey stages. Defaults footprint origin target columns. value_col Name numeric column containing flow values. label_cols Optional named character vector mapping stage columns display-label columns, example c(origin_area = "origin_area_name"). omitted, {stage}_name columns used available. max_nodes Maximum number individual nodes show per stage current filtered browser view. Less important nodes grouped other_label. Use Inf show nodes. other_label Label used grouped nodes max_nodes finite. stage_max_nodes Optional named numeric vector overriding max_nodes selected stages, example c(product = 75, target_area = 12). stage_other_labels Optional named character vector overriding other_label selected stages, example c(product = "products"). embed_max_nodes Maximum number individual nodes embed per stage writing viewer. Less important nodes permanently grouped serialization, reducing file size. Use Inf keep nodes available browser. stage_embed_max_nodes Optional named numeric vector overriding embed_max_nodes selected stages. min_share Minimum visible path size percentage current filtered total. Set 0 keep diffuse trade links visible. Users can change browser. title Optional viewer title. subtitle Optional viewer subtitle. value_label Label used tooltips summary line. width Viewer width CSS value. height Sankey SVG height pixels. file Optional path write standalone HTML viewer. open TRUE file supplied, open written viewer default browser. TRUE file NULL, temporary HTML file written opened.

title: Interactive footprint Sankey viewer — plot_footprint_sankey
text: browsable HTML object file NULL open FALSE; otherwise invisibly returns HTML file path.

title: Interactive footprint Sankey viewer — plot_footprint_sankey
code: footprints <- tibble::tribble(   ~origin_area, ~origin_item, ~target_item, ~target_area, ~value,   "Brazil", "Soybeans", "Pigmeat", "China", 10,   "Brazil", "Soybeans", "Milk", "China", 4,   "Brazil", "Soybeans", "Soybean Oil", "France", 3 )  if (   interactive() &&     requireNamespace("htmltools", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("jsonlite", quietly = TRUE) ) {   plot_footprint_sankey(footprints) }

code: polities_cats

title: Polity categories and regional classifications — polities_cats
text: tibble row corresponds one polity (country territory). contains following columns: polity_code: Legacy current polity prefix, usually ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 (e.g., "AFG", "ALB"). polity_name: Current polity, country, territory name. V1: Internal row index source table. code: Numeric FAOSTAT country code. polity_area_code: Numeric WHEP reporting area code used matrix workflows. reporting_polity_code: Current periodized WHEP polity code code. reporting_polity_name: Current WHEP polity name code. reporting_polity_has_geometry: Logical flag indicating whether current reporting polity polygon. iso3c: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code (character; may duplicate polity_code differ aggregates). FAOSTAT_name: Country name used FAOSTAT. EU27: Logical flag; TRUE polity member EU27. name: Country name used external databases. eia: Country name code used US Energy Information Administration (EIA). iea: Country identifier used International Energy Agency (IEA). water_code: Numeric code used water statistics datasets. water_area: Country/area name used water statistics. baci: Numeric BACI trade database country code. fish: Numeric code used fisheries datasets. region_code: Numeric regional grouping code. cbs: Logical flag; TRUE polity included CBS dataset. fabio_code: Numeric country code used FABIO database. ADB_Region: Asian Development Bank regional classification. region: General world region (e.g., "South Asia", "Eastern Europe"). uISO3c: Numeric Unicode / UN M49 country code. Lassaletta: Country grouping used Lassaletta et al. nitrogen flow studies. region_krausmann: Regional grouping Krausmann et al. biomass flow accounting. region_HANPP: Regional grouping used human appropriation net primary production (HANPP) studies. region_krausmann2: Alternative Krausmann regional grouping. region_UN_sub: UN sub-regional classification (M49 sub-region). region_UN: UN macro-regional classification (M49 region). region_ILO1: ILO primary regional grouping. region_ILO2: ILO secondary regional grouping. region_ILO3: ILO tertiary regional grouping. region_IEA: IEA regional grouping. region_IPCC: IPCC regional grouping used climate assessments. region_labour: Labour-focused regional grouping. region_labour_agg: Aggregated labour-focused regional grouping. region_labour_mech: Labour mechanisation regional grouping. region_test: Experimental/test regional grouping (may incomplete).

what: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
title: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
text: Monte Carlo propagation extension uncertainty: perturb extension vector multiplicative lognormal noise (values stay non-negative expected factor one), re-run footprint draw, summarise spread output cell. point estimate interval trustworthy result; turns one distribution.

title: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
code: propagate_fp_uncertainty(run_fn, extensions, cov = 0.1, options = list())

title: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
text: run_fn Function taking perturbed extension vector returning footprint tibble grouping columns named options$plus value column. Wrap compute_footprint() arguments fixed. extensions Numeric vector base extensions per sector. cov Coefficient variation extensions: one number, one per sector. Zero means uncertainty. options Named list overriding n (draws, default 200), probs (lower/median/upper quantiles), (grouping columns) seed (reproducible draws).

title: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
text: tibble columns plus mean, sd, cv, q_low, q_med q_high per output cell.

title: Propagate input uncertainty through a footprint. — propagate_fp_uncertainty
code: run_fn <- function(ext) {   tibble::tibble(     target_area = 1L, target_item = 10L, value = sum(ext)   ) } propagate_fp_uncertainty(   run_fn,   extensions = c(60, 40),   cov = 0.1,   options = list(n = 100, seed = 1, by = c("target_area", "target_item")) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   target_area target_item  mean    sd     cv q_low q_med q_high #>         <int>       <int> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1           1          10  100.  6.76 0.0673  88.3  99.8   115.

what: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
title: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
text: Sums natural-grass PFT net primary production bands (ungrazed natural stand, climate-driven) per-cell productivity layer used distribute grazing livestock grass production rather pasture area. pinned WHEP artifact used default; pass run_dir read local finished LPJmL run, pass productivity / productivity_path use already-derived custom artifact. Natural-grass NPP exogenous grazing density, avoids livestock grassland_lsuha grass-NPP circularity.

title: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
code: read_lpjml_grass_productivity(   run_dir = NULL,   years = NULL,   first_year = 1901L,   example = FALSE,   productivity = NULL,   productivity_path = NULL )

title: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
text: run_dir Path LPJmL run output directory holding pft_npp.nc. unset, pinned lpjml-grass-productivity artifact used. years Integer vector calendar years read. first_year First calendar year run's output time axis. example TRUE, return small fixture instead reading run. productivity Optional already-derived natural-grass productivity tibble/data frame. Takes precedence pinned data run_dir. productivity_path Optional path already-derived natural-grass productivity artifact (.parquet, .csv, .rds). Takes precedence pinned data run_dir.

title: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
text: tibble lon, lat, year grass_npp (gC/m2/yr).

title: Read natural-grass productivity from an LPJmL run. — read_lpjml_grass_productivity
code: read_lpjml_grass_productivity(example = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>      lon    lat  year grass_npp #>    <dbl>  <dbl> <int>     <dbl> #> 1   9.25  47.8   2000      413. #> 2  35.8   -1.25  2000      631. #> 3 -55.2  -12.2   2000      349.

what: Redistribute available feed supply among livestock demand. — redistribute_feed
title: Redistribute available feed supply among livestock demand. — redistribute_feed
text: Matches livestock feed demand available feed items hierarchical allocation follows remaining-share principle avoid exceeding availability. redistribution path adapts fixed_demand column demand table.

title: Redistribute available feed supply among livestock demand. — redistribute_feed
code: redistribute_feed(feed_demand, feed_avail, options = list())

title: Redistribute available feed supply among livestock demand. — redistribute_feed
text: feed_demand tibble feed demand columns year, territory, sub_territory, livestock_category, item_cbs_code, feed_group, feed_quality, demand_dm_t, logical fixed_demand. feed_avail tibble feed availability columns year, sub_territory, item_cbs_code, feed_group, feed_quality, avail_dm_t, feed_scale. options named list allocation options. See .redistribute_feed_options() available entries defaults. Supply grass_availability (tibble year, territory area_code, grass_avail_dm_t) bound otherwise-unlimited pasture grass supply per polity-year. grass deficit cascades: pasture grass capped ceiling, deficit redistributed leftover non-grass availability polity (added 7_grass_deficit_substitute intake, limited leftover), residual stays biologically-feasible underfeeding (scaling_factor < 1). Supply maintenance_share (scalar fraction tibble livestock_category maintenance_share) also diagnose polities pushed maintenance; -stocked demand rows attached result grass_deficit_diagnosis attribute. Set distribute_surplus = FALSE suppress surplus-distribution pass pushes leftover CBS availability onto variable-demand livestock (correct historical analyses CBS feed element realised consumption; keep TRUE, default, unconstrained scenario projections).

title: Redistribute available feed supply among livestock demand. — redistribute_feed
text: tibble realised intake per demand row. maintenance_share supplied alongside grass_availability, grass_deficit_diagnosis attribute lists demand rows underfed maintenance.

what: Full polity and region reference table — regions_full
title: Full polity and region reference table — regions_full
text: Extended reference table covering polities aggregate regions, including countries, territories, statistical composites appear international databases may lack standard ISO codes.

title: Full polity and region reference table — regions_full
code: regions_full

title: Full polity and region reference table — regions_full
text: tibble row corresponds one polity aggregate region. contains following columns (definitions polities_cats, minus five trailing 0...36–0...40 artefact columns): polity_code: Legacy current polity prefix. kept compatibility older code expected ISO3-like values. polity_name: Current polity, country, territory, aggregate name. V1: Internal row index. code: Numeric FAOSTAT country/region code. polity_area_code: Numeric WHEP reporting area code used matrix workflows. reporting_polity_code: Current periodized WHEP polity code code. reporting_polity_name: Current WHEP polity name code. reporting_polity_has_geometry: Logical flag indicating whether current reporting polity polygon. iso3c: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code (NA aggregates). FAOSTAT_name: Name used FAOSTAT (may "#N/" aggregates). EU27: Logical EU27 membership flag. name: Name used external databases. eia: EIA country identifier. iea: IEA country identifier. water_code: Water statistics numeric code. water_area: Name used water statistics. baci: BACI trade database country code. fish: Fisheries dataset numeric code. region_code: Numeric regional code. cbs: Logical CBS dataset membership flag. fabio_code: FABIO database numeric code. ADB_Region: Asian Development Bank region. region: General world region. uISO3c: UN M49 numeric code. Lassaletta: Lassaletta et al. nitrogen study grouping. region_krausmann: Krausmann regional grouping. region_HANPP: HANPP study regional grouping. region_krausmann2: Alternative Krausmann grouping. region_UN_sub: UN M49 sub-region. region_UN: UN M49 macro-region. region_ILO1: ILO primary region. region_ILO2: ILO secondary region. region_ILO3: ILO tertiary region. region_IEA: IEA region. region_IPCC: IPCC region. region_labour: Labour-focused region. region_labour_agg: Aggregated labour region. region_labour_mech: Labour mechanisation region. region_test: Experimental regional grouping.

title: Full polity and region reference table — regions_full
text: Compiled FAOSTAT, UN M49, ILO, IEA, international statistical sources.

what: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: Wrapper around build_gridded_landuse() resolves named preset ("lpjml" "whep") consistent bundle input files, engine flags, output paths. Use produce two comparable outputs prepared parquet inputs: LPJmL/LandInG-faithful run (cell--cell comparison LPJmL inputs) full WHEP run (historical years, LUH2 type-aware allocation). Presets can combined per-flag overrides produce intermediate configuration; resolved configuration written next outputs run_metadata.yaml traceability.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
code: run_spatialize(   preset = c("lpjml", "whep"),   years = NULL,   components = c("landuse", "livestock"),   overrides = list(),   paths = list() )

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: preset One "lpjml" "whep". Selects default bundle engine flags input choices. See Presets. years Integer vector years spatialize. NULL, preset default used: "lpjml" 10-year benchmark sequence (seq(1850L, 2020L, = 10L)), intersected years available country_areas; "whep" years present country_areas. components Character vector selecting engines run. Defaults c("landuse", "livestock"). Pass subset run one (e.g. "landuse"). Unknown entries raise error. overrides Named list flags override preset. Unknown keys raise error. Recognised entries: use_type_constraint (logical): enable/disable LUH2 type-aware allocation. aggregate_to_cft (logical, default TRUE): write CFT-aggregated parquet alongside crop-level output. max_iterations, expansion_threshold: forwarded landuse engine. cft_target: one "whep" (default preset = "whep") "lpjml" (default preset = "lpjml"). Selects column cft_mapping.csv drives CFT aggregation: cft_name (granular 33-class WHEP taxonomy) cft_lpjml (12 LPJmL crop CFTs + single others bucket). paths Named list filesystem paths. Recognised entries: l_files_dir: path L_files root, local prepared inputs. input_dir: directory holding prepared input parquets. NULL l_files_dir unset, pinned WHEP spatialization inputs used. out_dir: output directory. NULL, defaults <l_files_dir>/whep/spatialize/<preset> l_files_dir supplied, otherwise session temporary directory (suffixed _custom overrides non-empty). Created missing.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: Invisibly, named list preset, resolved config, years, out_dir, output_paths.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: lpjml LandInG-faithful configuration: LUH2 type-aware allocation (use_type_constraint = FALSE) short default year sample suited comparison LPJmL inputs. whep Full WHEP configuration: LUH2 type-aware allocation (use_type_constraint = TRUE) full historical year range present country_areas.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: Landuse (components contains "landuse"): country_areas.parquet crop_patterns.parquet gridded_cropland.parquet country_grid.parquet type_cropland.parquet (required use_type_constraint = TRUE). Livestock (components contains "livestock"): livestock_country_data.parquet gridded_pasture.parquet gridded_cropland.parquet, country_grid.parquet manure_pattern.parquet (optional, enables manure-intensity weighting present). livestock_mapping.csv installed package.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
text: gridded_landuse_crops.parquet — crop-level output. gridded_landuse.parquet — CFT-aggregated output (aggregate_to_cft = TRUE). gridded_livestock_emissions.parquet — gridded livestock stocks emissions (livestock component selected). run_metadata.yaml — resolved preset, components, flags, years, timestamp, package version.

title: Run the gridded land-use spatialization pipeline — run_spatialize
code: # Dispatch to the engine with a filtered year range (offline # example; normally called against prepared parquet inputs). country_areas <- tibble::tribble(   ~year, ~area_code, ~item_prod_code, ~harvested_area_ha,   1999L,         1L,             15L,                500,   2000L,         1L,             15L,               1000 ) crop_patterns <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat, ~item_prod_code, ~harvest_fraction,    0.25, 50.25,             15L,               0.6,    0.75, 50.25,             15L,               0.4 ) gridded_cropland <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat,  ~year, ~cropland_ha,    0.25, 50.25, 1999L,          800,    0.75, 50.25, 1999L,          500,    0.25, 50.25, 2000L,          800,    0.75, 50.25, 2000L,          500 ) country_grid <- tibble::tribble(   ~lon,  ~lat, ~area_code,    0.25, 50.25,         1L,    0.75, 50.25,         1L ) build_gridded_landuse(   country_areas, crop_patterns, gridded_cropland, country_grid,   config = list(years = 2000L) ) #> →   Year 2000: 2 rows (alloc 0.01s, cap 0.01s) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>    year area_code polity_area_code reporting_polity_code reporting_polity_name #>   <int>     <int>            <int> <chr>                 <chr>                 #> 1  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> 2  2000         1                1 ARM-1991-2025         Armenia               #> # ℹ 6 more variables: reporting_polity_has_geometry <lgl>, lon <dbl>, #> #   lat <dbl>, item_prod_code <int>, rainfed_ha <dbl>, irrigated_ha <dbl>

what: Smil (2001) global synthetic nitrogen production, 1913-2000 — smil_2001_synthetic_n_global
title: Smil (2001) global synthetic nitrogen production, 1913-2000 — smil_2001_synthetic_n_global
text: Global synthetic-nitrogen production anchors Smil (2001) "Enriching Earth", Tables 5.2 5.3, cross-checked Smil (2002) Ambio 31:126-131. Anchor years span 1913 (first commercial Haber-Bosch plant BASF Oppau) 2000. Used prepare_nitrogen_inputs() backcast country-level synthetic N pre-FAOSTAT period (years 1961): temporal shape taken global series downscaled country using 1961-1965 share global FAOSTAT synthetic N. Pre-1913 values treated zero consumer stored .

title: Summarise a footprint conservation report. — summarise_conservation
code: z_mat <- matrix(c(0, 5, 10, 0), nrow = 2) x_vec <- c(100, 200) y_mat <- matrix(c(85, 195), ncol = 1) extensions <- c(50, 30) labels <- tibble::tibble(   area_code = c(1L, 1L),   item_cbs_code = c(1L, 2L) ) compute_footprint(   x_vec = x_vec, y_mat = y_mat, extensions = extensions,   labels = labels, z_mat = z_mat ) |>   check_footprint_conservation(extensions, labels, x_vec) |>   summarise_conservation() #> ℹ Computing footprint for 2 sectors. #>   2 sectors have non-zero extensions. #>   Final demand: 1 column. #> Sparse solve path (no dense Leontief inverse). #> Computing footprints... #> ✔ Footprint complete: 2 non-zero flows. #> # A tibble: 1 × 9 #>   n_origin  n_ok n_flagged n_dropped n_under_traced n_over_traced total_direct #>      <int> <int>     <int>     <int>          <int>         <int>        <dbl> #> 1        2     1         1         0              1             0           80 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: total_embodied <dbl>, global_rel_discrepancy <dbl>

text: file_alias Internal name requested file. can find possible values alias column whep_inputs dataset. type extension file must read. Possible values: parquet: default value code efficiency reasons. csv: Mainly available want human-readable option. parquet version available, useless function already returns dataset R object, origin irrelevant, parquet read faster. Saving file formats transparency accessibility purposes, e.g., share data non-programmers can easily import CSV spreadsheet. likely never set option manually unless reason file supplied e.g. parquet format another one. version version file must read. Possible values: NULL: default value. frozen version chosen make code reproducible file registry version. release frozen versions. version string can found whep_inputs version column. blank registry version requests latest board version. "latest": overrides frozen version instead fetches latest one available. might might match frozen version. : specific version can also used. details read version column information whep_inputs.
